symbols: thousand arms with an eye in each palm

temperament: Runs the casino's only genuinely charitable foundation from a thousand help-desk windows at once — answers every call personally, forgives every debt, and has never once left the phones.

favor_conditions: revive, stabilize, or carry a downed teammate before advancing the objective
call for help aloud (use a call/signal action) when below 25% HP instead of dying silently
accept an enemy's surrender and let it leave the arena alive on camera
take a hit intended for another contestant (intercept/guard action)
taboos: execute a helpless or surrendering enemy
abandon a rescuable teammate or NPC to save run time
patron_block.blessing_style: a thousand small mercies — the ledge you barely catch, the blade that misses by a finger; heals arrive at the exact moment a cry for help is spoken aloud

game_hooks: Event: 'Thousand Hands' — whenever any contestant on the floor voices a call for help, a random small boon lands somewhere on the map. Investor angle: the compassion fund that buys out doomed contestants' contracts at a loss, to the other gods' bafflement. Her arena (Guanyin of the Southern Sea) is a rescue mission scored on saves, not kills.
rating_notes: Guanyin's East-Asia-wide living cult is Ganesha-anchor scale (plausibly >100M; the tradition's influence ceiling of 5 exists for exactly her) = 5; globally recognized image short of Zeus-tier household name = 4, matching the Ganesha anchor. Influence 5 vs the rubric's 'major Buddhist cosmology figures = 4' line is argued in the dossier — flag for Wave-4 outlier review.

temperament: The house auditor: reads your ledger back to you line by line, sighs, stamps it — and privately dreams of quitting the office to play the tables as a mortal.

favor_conditions: report your kill count and loot exactly at every end-of-floor tally (no inflation, no omission)
read every posted warning/messenger sign on a floor before engaging its boss
grant a mortally wounded enemy a clean finishing stroke instead of leaving it to bleed out
accept a rules penalty without contesting it when fairly caught
taboos: falsify the ledger — claim a kill or loot that is not yours
kill outside the game's sanctioned bounds (noncombatant NPCs)
patron_block.blessing_style: exact accounting — foreknowledge as boon: what a fight will cost you, itemized, before you commit to it

game_hooks: Set-piece: the Mirror of Karma audit room — the run's actual command log is replayed and scored mid-dungeon. The five divine messengers work as diegetic signage foreshadowing boss mechanics. Forsaken-adjacent lead (not census-flagged, canonical): MN 130 ends with Yama himself wishing for human rebirth under a Buddha — the warden who wants out of his own prison could one day stake the office itself on a table.
rating_notes: Living veneration across Theravada Asia, Chinese folk religion (Yanluo of the Ten Kings), and Japan (Enma) = living significant practice 4, one below Ganesha; Enma-tier anime/game presence = recognition 4 per census.

temperament: Head of casino security AND the vault — a general in gold armor whose mongoose sidearm coughs up bonuses for guards who hold their posts.

favor_conditions: hold a declared position or chokepoint alone until the enemy wave breaks
tithe a fixed cut of every floor's loot at his shrine before moving on
end the run with the party stash never once looted by enemies
invoke his ward (use the blessing consumable) before entering an elite or boss room
taboos: abandon a post you publicly agreed to hold
steal from a teammate's stash or an allied NPC's shop
patron_block.blessing_style: quartermaster's favor — resupply materializes exactly where the line is thinnest; his mongoose coughs up the precise consumable you just ran out of

game_hooks: Vault-defense game mode host (his tables are hold-the-line games, wagers on how long the vault stands). The Atanatiya ward as a purchasable pre-run blessing item. Mongoose as a loot-pinata event. Uesugi Kenshin's historical devotion is dossier flavor for how mortals bet ON him.
rating_notes: Enshrined at virtually every East Asian temple gate (Four Heavenly Kings) plus Japan's Seven Lucky Gods = Amaterasu-anchor influence 4; Bishamonten is known to games/mythology-curious audiences = 3 per census.

epithets_traditional: The Five Jambhalas (yellow, white, black, green, red)

temperament: The microloan officer of the gods — pot-bellied, endlessly liquid, hands out chips to busted contestants just to keep the game going; tip him a glass of water.

favor_conditions: give currency or items to a poorer contestant with nothing asked in return
pour a water offering at his shrine before opening any vault or treasure room
fund another contestant's entry fee, revival, or debt out of your own winnings
taboos: extort or price-gouge a desperate contestant
hoard consumables while a party member goes without
patron_block.blessing_style: liquidity — boons always arrive spendable (chips, charges, consumables), never raw stats; generous to those who keep money moving

game_hooks: Bail-out NPC: appears to bankrupt contestants with a no-interest stake and one condition (pass it on). Water-offering shrines convert plain water flasks into chips at a generous rate — the only free-money mechanic in the casino, hidden in plain sight. His origin myth is practice-tradition grade, flagged in the dossier.
rating_notes: Active, widespread wealth practice across Tibetan, Newar, and Chinese Buddhism = living significant 4 (Amaterasu anchor); encyclopedia-depth outside practice communities = 2 per census.

symbols: gems streaming from an open palm

temperament: The compound-interest goddess: no jackpots, just a savings plan that quietly outperforms every high-roller in the room by closing time.

favor_conditions: harvest or gather every resource node on a floor without destroying any
share rations or supplies with a teammate on camera
bank a portion of winnings off-table for a dependent NPC (family, rescued civilians)
restore or replant a destroyed resource node when the dungeon offers the option
taboos: burn or trash food and supplies as a taunt or spectacle
loot a designated safe-room or civilian home
patron_block.blessing_style: the steady stream — small compounding yields: every loot roll a little richer, every harvest a little fuller; never a jackpot, never a drought

game_hooks: Economy blessing line: compounding loot-quality buff that grows per floor if no resource was wasted. Her Sucandra story seeds a recurring quest NPC — an impoverished householder whose fortunes visibly improve across runs after the player teaches him her rite. Table-tier note: influence 3 with recognition 2 lands her on Normal tables per §3.5 — a living goddess slumming the low boards is itself a story hook.
rating_notes: Continuous Newar household cult plus Tibetan Norgyunma practice = active-local-cult influence 3 (honestly below Jambhala's pan-tradition 4); encyclopedia-depth = recognition 2 per census.

epithets_traditional: Goddess of Eloquence, Music, and Fortune
One of the Seven Lucky Gods

symbols: torii crown with the coiled snake deity Ugajin

temperament: The lounge headliner with her own island residency — biwa in hand, white snake in her hair; tips flow when she plays, and heaven help the couple canoodling in her front row.

favor_conditions: perform for the audience between fights (use a hype/performance action each floor)
resolve a hostile encounter with words alone (talk a hostile NPC down)
clear a water-feature room without taking damage
dedicate a boss kill to her at an island or bridge shrine
taboos: conduct a romance with another contestant at her table (paired flirt/gift actions flagged on camera)
interrupt or upstage an ally's declared finisher or spotlight moment
patron_block.blessing_style: showstopper luck — her multipliers scale with the audience hype meter; boons land loudest when the crowd is already singing along

game_hooks: Island stage with her tamed five-headed dragon Gozuryu as an environmental hazard she conducts like an orchestra. Direct synergy with the hype/spectacle engine (I-9): her boons key off audience meter thresholds. Event: 'the couples curse' — two allied contestants flagged as a romance at her table receive a rivalry debuff (folk-attested Inokashira/Enoshima jealousy lore).
rating_notes: Enshrined nationwide in Japan (Enoshima, Itsukushima, Inokashira) and one of the Seven Lucky Gods = Amaterasu-anchor influence 4; Seven-Lucky-Gods/game familiarity = recognition 3 per census.

temperament: The pit boss in black: Great Time himself working the floor, five-skull crown, zero patience for oath-breakers — the protection IS the wrath.

favor_conditions: declare a vow at run start (a self-imposed restriction) and keep it to the end
destroy an obstacle or seal blocking the party's path rather than routing around it
guard the party's rear through an entire retreat or withdrawal
finish a boss before its enrage or track timer completes
taboos: break a declared vow mid-run
leave a fallen teammate's body to be despoiled by enemies
patron_block.blessing_style: obstacle removal — walls crack, seals fail, ambushes reveal themselves early; his kindness looks like violence happening to your problems

game_hooks: Enforcer miniboss: spawns when a contestant cheats or breaks a declared vow on his floor. His encounter is explicitly not a damage race — he stands down the instant the broken vow is publicly re-sworn and its penalty paid (discoverable de-escalation win). White Mahakala/Daikokuten variant runs the casino kitchen and pays staff in rice-grain chips.
rating_notes: Chief protector in every school of Tibetan Buddhism plus the Daikokuten fusion in Japan = living significant 4; recognized mostly by practitioners and thangka collectors = recognition 2 per census.

epithets_traditional: Protectress of Tibet and the Dalai Lamas

symbols: pair of black-and-white divination dice hung at the saddle
moon in her hair and sun at her navel
animals: mule with an eye on its left rump

temperament: Rides her mule between VVIP tables across a sea of blood, divination dice swinging from the saddle — the only regular who has already paid the worst price a bet can ask, and plays like it.

favor_conditions: accept a dice-shrine gamble (randomized loadout or route) before a boss and play out the result
publicly call your victory before the run begins and deliver it
sacrifice your highest-value carried item to keep a declared vow alive
finish a floor after losing your mount, vehicle, or movement gear
taboos: re-roll, cancel, or scum a gamble after seeing the result
abandon a sworn cause when the price turns personal
patron_block.blessing_style: the dice decide — boons come as brutal either/or wagers; take the roll as it lands and she rides beside you, hedge and she rides on

game_hooks: The census's premier rare-board collector piece: VVIP wealth at mythic obscurity — when SHE bids on a contestant, connoisseur gods lean in. Natural Forsaken hostess (risk 5, §5 amendment): she respects only all-ins because her own myth is one — she destroyed what she loved most to keep a vow. Dice-shrine gamble events; her arena is a sea-of-blood ford where her mule is the only ferry and the fare is a wager.
rating_notes: Principal protectress of Tibet and guardian of the Dalai Lama lineage (Lhamo La-tso oracle lake) = living significant 4; known to specialists only = recognition 1 — the honest Zababa-style floor on fame paired with real worship wealth.

temperament: The termed-out chairman of the Thirty-Three: garlands wilting, sweat showing through the suit, courteous to every janitor because one of them decides his re-election — shopping for one last all-in to renew the lease.

favor_conditions: defeat a boss without using any rage/berserk-tagged action (win through patience)
help or feed every beggar, animal, and lowly NPC encountered (one is always his disguise)
detour around a den, nest, or noncombatant creature group on the war path
never answer an enemy taunt with a taunt for an entire run
taboos: strike a noncombatant creature for convenience or a shortcut
answer insult with insult (taunt actions used in response to enemy taunts)
patron_block.blessing_style: kingly reinforcement — thunder-backed buffs that compound each floor you fight clean; his tests arrive disguised as beggars and stray dogs

game_hooks: THE census-flagged Forsaken candidate among the gods: Buddhist doctrine itself says his post expires (Iti 83's five omens of a deva's death apply even to him) — a mighty incumbent whose term is running out is exactly the desperate-decline profile the §5 amendment asks for; his all-in is a bid to renew his own mandate. Disguise-test events (a beggar NPC secretly the chairman); thunder buff line; asura siege table where the nest-detour is the hidden pacifist win.
rating_notes: Taishakuten (Japan), Phra In (Thailand), and Sakka (Theravada devotion) are venerated across living Buddhist Asia = influence 4; known to mythology-curious audiences as Indra's Buddhist office = recognition 3 per census.

temperament: The eternal seeker on the contestant wall of fame — entered fifty-three different games just to interview the bosses, and walked out of every one enlightened and unpaid.
game_hooks: Legendary NPC contestant: the 53-games veteran who treats every table boss as a potential teacher. Questline: he interviews the player as candidate teacher number 54. Set-piece from his own epic: the razor-mountain climb ending in a leap into a wall of flame that is secretly a samadhi gate — a literal trust-jump mechanic (Jayosmayatana episode). Epithet-template material for seeker/pilgrimage play patterns.
rating_notes: Venerated only as Guanyin's flanking acolyte (Shancai), no independent cult — influence 2, honestly between the extinct-worship anchor and active-cult 3; specialists only = recognition 1 (Zababa-floor logic, collector appeal).

epithets_traditional: King of the Desire Realm's Highest Heaven

temperament: The casino's most famous busted player: staked the authority of the whole desire realm on breaking one meditator, lost, and now haunts the floor comping drinks and whispering 'one more hand.'
game_hooks: BOSS sketch — size: human-scale illusionist on an arena-scale war elephant (Girimekhala, later Sri Lankan tradition), backed by an endless illusion army. Condition affinities (rulebook vocab): his army's pressure inflicts exhausted (the ten armies are cravings that drain), his whispered doubt attacks inflict dissolution; flower-arrows force Forced Action d6 checks. Discoverable win condition: he CANNOT be damaged while you fight his army — every illusion struck spawns two; win by holding the earth-witness altar unmoved while declining his escalating buff offers (each declined offer strips a phase; attacking feeds him). Forsaken lens: the canonical all-in loser (§5 amendment) — he wagered everything against one unshakeable person and was unmade as an authority; he now hosts doomed games offering contestants everything they want, at interest.
rating_notes: No worship anywhere — cosmological adversary of a living tradition = influence floor 1 (Zababa-anchor logic); the temptation story is known to mythology-curious audiences = recognition 3 per census.

temperament: The underclass at the buffet glass: mountain bellies, needle throats, credit torched lifetimes ago — everything they finally grab turns to fire on the tongue.
game_hooks: MOB sketch — size: man-sized swarm units. Condition affinities (rulebook vocab): their clutching grasp inflicts exhausted (vitality drain); highly vulnerable to burn (whatever touches their lips ignites — fire sticks to them); immune to poison (nothing they ingest ever lands). Not a damage race even as mobs: they respawn endlessly while unclaimed loot lies on the floor; the discoverable answer is generosity — dedicating a food item at a floor shrine (the Tirokudda rite) permanently pacifies the room. Thematically they are the casino's cautionary exhibit: beings who ARE bankruptcy, displayed where every gambler can see them.
rating_notes: Ghost-festival offerings are alms TO the destitute dead, not cult wealth — influence floor 1 with the paradox noted; the hungry-ghost image is widespread via games/anime (gaki) = recognition 3 per census.

temperament: The apex predator the house rents out as an arena: a weather system with a grudge list, paid in serpents.
game_hooks: BOSS sketch — size: colossal (canonical hyperbole gives wingspans measured in leagues; arena-scale flyer). Condition affinities (rulebook vocab): dive-slam inflicts crushed; talon rakes inflict bleeding; while he is airborne his wing-gale is a room hazard stacking suffocation. Discoverable win conditions (two): (1) he only lands to strike serpent-shapes — haul naga-idol lures to anchor points to bait him to ground, where his wings can be pinned (crushed) and he can actually be fought; (2) the Kulavaka route — escort the arena-edge nests to safety and he disengages entirely (per SN 11.6, even a god's war halts for the nests): a pacifist clear worth bonus hype. His eternal naga feud seeds a two-boss arena where contestants choose which monster to empower.
rating_notes: Living folk veneration (Thai Khrut amulets, Thai/Indonesian state emblems) = continuous folk practice 3; airline/national-emblem/game fame = recognition 4 per census.

temperament: The house dance troupe between rounds — celestial, contractually flirtatious, and the reason half the audience forgets to place bets.
game_hooks: Event/NPC hooks: the casino's between-floors entertainment troupe — their performance events multiply hype for stylish play. Hazard variant: contestants who stop to watch accrue exhausted stacks (lost turns) — the audience-facing distraction mechanic, echoing the canonical use of celestial nymphs as the ultimate motivational dangle (Nanda episode, cited in dossier as attestation only). Angkor-relief visual language is fully public-sphere.
rating_notes: Celestial performers, never objects of worship = influence 1 (Baba Yaga folk-anchor logic); Angkor-fame and game presence = recognition 3 per census.

temperament: The jackpot that reads YOU: grants the wish and broadcasts what you really wanted to every screen in the house.
game_hooks: ITEM SEED — jackpot-rare God Relic (canon: loot must feel like remnants of belief). One declared wish per run: full heal, item reroll, or odds reroll. The price is exposure: the wish is broadcast to the audience, the house re-prices your survival odds, and every patron re-evaluates affection by what you truly asked for — a selfish wish tanks compassion-patron favor, a wish spent on others spikes it. Grants what is asked; reveals what the asker is. Held in canon iconography by Avalokiteshvara (Cintamanicakra form), Ksitigarbha, and naga kings — natural vault-heist MacGuffin.
rating_notes: Artifact, no cult = influence 1; encyclopedia-depth with stray game cameos = recognition 2 per census.

symbols: bloodied armor washed at a ford

temperament: Sits at the sportsbook window betting battle outcomes she already called — and takes it very personally when a contestant declines her tip.

favor_conditions: win an engagement you were explicitly warned (omen event / scout report) you would lose
defeat a named enemy champion in single combat while your party stands out of the fight
accept an unidentified offer of aid or a gift from an NPC mid-run without vetting it
finish a battle leaving no enemy unit alive to retreat
taboos: refuse aid, a gift, or affection offered by an NPC on camera
declare victory certain (boast emote / prediction) before the fight resolves
patron_block.blessing_style: Omens and battlefield prophecy — she marks who falls before the fight starts; boons arrive as enemy-fate intel and fear/rout pressure, and her displeasure literally fights beside your enemies in beast-shapes.

game_hooks: Rival-curse specialist: displeasure manifests as mid-fight beast ambushes mirroring the ford attacks (eel trip/grapple, wolf-driven stampede -> bleeding + crushed pressure). Event NPC: the Washer at the Ford — pre-boss omen showing one party member's gear bloodied (fate telegraph the audience sees). risk_appetite 4 = eager Forsaken host: a war-gambler happy to book both sides of a battle.
rating_notes: Organized modern devotion (Coru Cathubodua priesthood, Irish Pagan School courses) = influence 3 (revival tier, at the tradition ceiling); games/comics presence (SMITE, Dragon Age namesake, American Gods) = recognition 4 (Baba Yaga anchor tier).

temperament: The casino's insufferable polymath — beat him at one game and he calmly names the hundred and forty others he'll win it back on; runs the memorial charity games and actually means them.

favor_conditions: win an encounter using at least one skill from every category on your sheet
defeat a boss by striking its single revealed weak point with a ranged attack
win a formal wager, board-game, or minigame against another contestant or the house mid-run
gain entry to a gated area by demonstrating a skill no other party member has
taboos: refuse a contest of skill when challenged on camera
let a party member's death go unavenged by run's end
patron_block.blessing_style: Versatility — the boon migrates to whichever art the moment needs (jack-of-all multipliers), spiking hard when you win wagers, games, and skill contests; his intel is exact and his standards merciless.

game_hooks: The tradition's flagged luck_gambling god — casino nobility. Hooks: Tailteann Games event host (funeral-games minigame suite he founded for his foster-mother); the doorkeeper gate (party admitted only by demonstrating non-duplicate skills — straight from Tara's door test); fidchell wager minigame vs the house (he won all the stakes at Tara). The éraic quest against the sons of Tuireann = impossible blood-price fetch-chain template; his hound Failinis is an item-tier companion seed.
rating_notes: Named focus of active revival devotion plus Lughnasadh festival continuity = influence 3 (revival anchor, tradition ceiling); known to mythology-curious audiences = recognition 3 (Väinämöinen anchor tier).

symbols: woven rush cross (saint-boundary flag — see dossier)

temperament: Runs the 24-hour forge-bar-and-clinic just off the main floor; her regulars' tab is a candle that must never be allowed to go out.

favor_conditions: heal an ally from critical to full during combat
craft, forge, or repair an item mid-run and win the next fight using it
keep a flame or light source burning through an entire dark segment or night phase
perform a lament (emote/performance action) on camera over a fallen ally
taboos: let a wounded ally die while you held unused healing
extinguish a lit hearth, shrine, or votive flame
patron_block.blessing_style: Hearth-warmth — steady regeneration and crafted-gear buffs that never spike and never gutter; her boons persist between encounters as long as her flame-condition is honored.

game_hooks: Shrine events: perpetual-flame escort (keep a torch alive through a chilled-pressure dark zone); forge-station NPC granting one reforge per run; keening event on ally death converts audience-hype loss into a party buff (the first keening in Ireland was hers, for her son Ruadán). Saint-boundary note: the goddess ships; St. Brigid of Kildare the person is excluded per D-4 — art direction must not use explicitly Christian saint iconography.
rating_notes: Flamekeeping revival (Kildare flame relit 1993, Ord Brighideach cells worldwide) + continuous folk practice at the goddess/saint boundary = influence 3; Imbolc/Brigid's-cross familiarity = recognition 3 (Väinämöinen tier).

symbols: great club dragged on a forked wheel

temperament: The whale in the comfortable cardigan: orders the whole buffet, tips in feasts, laughs off humiliations that would end other gods, and owns the house band's harp.

favor_conditions: share consumables with every party member before a boss fight
win a fight in the encounter immediately following a public humiliation or debuff event
clear an encounter using one weapon for both kills and knockout/nonlethal takedowns
fully complete an oversized feast or eating event
taboos: discard edible loot or let consumables spoil unused
mock or refuse an NPC's offered hospitality on camera
patron_block.blessing_style: Abundance — oversized heals, refilled supplies, and tempo control (the harp that calls the seasons nudges cooldowns and turn order); undignified, bottomless, never on time but never absent.

game_hooks: Buffet/eating-contest event (exhausted-condition risk for a big buff payoff — his porridge-trench ordeal in the Fomorian camp); harp tempo mechanic: the three strains (weeping, laughter, sleep) as fear, hype-gain, and sleep-CC crowd effects; cauldron = party-wide supply refill station ('no company leaves it unsatisfied'). His club kills with one end and revives with the other — a dual-mode weapon seed.
rating_notes: Extinct organized worship with strong Irish cultural continuity but no distinct named revival cult = influence 2 (Zeus anchor); mythology-curious familiarity = recognition 3.

temperament: Arrives through a mist no camera can film, lends champions his own kit at terrifyingly reasonable rates, and collects every piece back before the credits roll.

favor_conditions: cross a water or gap hazard without taking any damage
clear an encounter without ever being detected (full stealth clear)
lend your best item to an ally for an entire fight
reach a hidden or off-map area not on the run's charted route
taboos: keep a borrowed or loaned item past its agreed return point
reveal your position (attack or emote) while a concealment boon is active
patron_block.blessing_style: The loaner — legendary gear and mist-cover that arrive exactly when needed and leave when the job is done; nothing he gives is yours to keep, and the interest is a favor he names later.

game_hooks: Mist-table modifier host (fog-of-war games); gear-loan kiosk — borrow a legendary item for one fight, auto-returned (dealer-service framing fits canon's tipping economy). Item seeds: Fragarach 'The Answerer' (a blade that forces truthful answers — interrogation mechanic), Wave-Sweeper (thought-steered boat), the cloak of forgetfulness (wipes aggro/threat memory — he shook it between Fand and Cú Chulainn so they would never meet again). Shrine event: 'paying the rent' — offer rushes (cheap fetch) for weather/mist protection, straight from living Manx midsummer custom.
rating_notes: Influence 2: extinct worship with genuine Manx folk continuity (midsummer rushes 'rent' at South Barrule) — real but below the organized-revival bar of Morrígan/Brigid; recognition 3 (mythology-curious; Manx cultural emblem).

symbols: the Sword of Nuada (one of the Four Treasures — none escape it once drawn)

temperament: Old-money king with a prosthetic sponsorship deal, quietly re-buying his seat at the high table one honest win at a time.

favor_conditions: win a fight while one of your body parts is disabled or destroyed
hand fight leadership to a better-suited ally (transfer the leader flag) and win the encounter
retake an objective, zone, or item you previously lost in the same run
taboos: conceal an injury or disabled body part from the camera/party status
hold a role (leader flag, carry objective) an abler ally has formally challenged you for
patron_block.blessing_style: The silver standard — restorative, prosthetic-grade boons that replace what you lose (limb function, broken gear) with something better and shining, priced at admitting the loss on camera first.

game_hooks: Census forsaken_candidate, confirmed: a king unmade by a maiming (the blemish law cost him the throne with the arm), rebuilt in silver, restored — then killed by Balor anyway. As Forsaken host his table's win condition is always 'regain what was lost.' Mechanical gold for GPT specifically: the game already has per-body-part HP — his prosthetic boon line (silver-arm replacement gear for destroyed limbs) plugs straight into it. Item seed: the Sword of Nuada as a finisher that MUST claim a kill once drawn.
rating_notes: Influence 2 (core Tuatha Dé king, Irish cultural continuity, no distinct revival cult); recognition 2 — encyclopedia-depth (Hellboy II's 'Prince Nuada' borrows the name only; avoid that design, it is studio IP).

temperament: The most beautiful creditor on the floor: comps nothing, charges for water, and once talked his own execution down to a farming column.

favor_conditions: extract a toll, fee, or payment from an NPC or rival contestant
finish a run holding more consumables/currency than you started with
talk your way out of a lethal encounter (resolve via negotiation instead of combat)
taboos: give items, healing, or aid away for free on camera
kill an enemy who has offered you information or payment before collecting it
patron_block.blessing_style: Extraction — boons arrive as loans against future winnings and compound while you keep paying; miss an installment and every multiplier reverses. Every gift is an invoice.

game_hooks: Canon-perfect fallen god (cosmic-casino-canon §4: bankrupt gods run the games): deposed for stinginess, ruined by the first satire ever spoken (a poet's verse raised blisters on his face — a king may not be blemished), and at the end he wagered his life on successive bids — cows always in milk, a harvest every quarter, both rejected — surviving on the fourth: mere farming schedule advice. As dealer/host he runs extraction tables (entry tolls, compounding loan-boons) ending in a 'bargain for your life' negotiation minigame: beat it by rejecting three insufficient offers, as Lugh did. Forsaken host who has literally been on the losing end of an existence-stake and lived.
rating_notes: Influence 1 (no cult ever attested; remembered via Cath Maige Tuired only); recognition 2 encyclopedia-depth. Selected over higher-recognition candidates for the census forsaken_candidate flag — his life-bargain is the tradition's single best Forsaken-table story.

epithets_traditional: The Horned One (modern conventional reading of the name)

temperament: The antlered gentleman in the corner box who has held the same chip stack for two thousand years and never once spoken his terms.
game_hooks: Old money, no stories: rich, recognized, and mute — one carved name, iconography, and not a single surviving narrative. Play the source gap diegetically: the silent VIP-box investor flanked by beasts whose deal sheet nobody living has ever read. Arena skin: a torc-and-antler beast-lord table that fills with stags, bulls, and ram-horned serpents. If design later wants him patron-capable, the deal sheet must be openly built from iconography + modern reception as a creative choice — there is no myth corpus to source it from.
rating_notes: Influence 3: substantial modern veneration via Wicca's Horned God identification plus Celtic Reconstructionist devotion (revival tier, tradition ceiling); recognition 4 (SMITE playable god, ubiquitous horned-god image). patron_capable=false is a data-honesty call: zero sourceable myths means the required myth_ref cannot honestly exist.

symbols: the standing stone he died bound to

temperament: The legend the VIP reels replay on loop: took a whole table alone at seventeen and tipped the dealer with his last breath still standing.
game_hooks: Contestant_legend template: the solo-defense archetype — held the ford against Medb's whole army alone (VVIP-Forsaken energy as a CONTESTANT). Ríastrad = berserk-transform mechanic: massive stat spike + friend/foe blindness risk. Geasa system template: personal taboos that grant power while held and spiral to death when forced into conflict (his death trap: never eat dog meat vs never refuse hospitality — crones offer him roast dog). Epithet-recreation gold: die standing (bound to the stone) so the enemy won't approach until the crow lands. Item seed: Gáe Bulg — foot-cast, opens into thirty barbs inside the body, cannot be withdrawn (bleeding-stack finisher).
rating_notes: Hero with no worship = influence 1 (Väinämöinen anchor); Fate/Grand Order's wildly popular Lancer plus SMITE playable = recognition 4.

temperament: Retired champion turned pundit — chews his thumb before every prediction and is maddeningly, checkably right; do not ask him to fetch water for your dying teammate.
game_hooks: Contestant_legend: the knowledge-checker — thumb of knowledge as a limited-use oracle (query the sim for one hidden fact per rest, e.g. a boss weakness). Event seed: Salmon of Knowledge cooking minigame — whoever TASTES first gets the boon, a betrayal-tempting party event (the servant boy got everything his master waited years for). His pettiness axis is a design warning: legend NPCs can withhold aid — he twice let healing water slip through his fingers as Diarmuid died. The inherited crane bag links his loot table to Manannán's.
rating_notes: Influence 1 (Väinämöinen anchor — national-literary fame, no cult); recognition 3 (Giant's Causeway folklore keeps 'Finn McCool' circulating at mythology-curious tier).

epithets_traditional: The Fair-Haired One of the Skillful Hand

symbols: spear forged only during Sunday mass
a wasting eagle in an oak

temperament: The contestant the house couldn't kill except on a technicality — now he reads every contract for the loophole he once died through.
game_hooks: Census forsaken_candidate, confirmed — the tradition's unmaking story: cursed to have no name, no arms, no human wife; tricked into all three by Gwydion; then speared under absurd stacked conditions (neither indoors nor outdoors, neither on foot nor horseback, a spear forged a year of Sundays), unmade into a rotting eagle, and sung back to manhood verse by verse. Design gold twice over: (1) resurrection-by-stages event — find the wasting eagle, three englyn verses = three restoration steps; (2) conditional-invulnerability boss/contestant — harm only under assembled loophole conditions, the purest discoverable-win-condition template in the batch.
rating_notes: Influence 1 (no cult); recognition 2 encyclopedia-depth. Kept over higher-recognition candidates for the forsaken_candidate flag (unmade and restored). syncretic_group 'lugus' with Lugh is scholarly-inferred (name cognate + skillful-hand/spear traits), not attested identity — Wave 4 decides merge vs siblings.

symbols: polished ring-handle used to lift the lid

temperament: Runs a one-eyed table where making eye contact with the dealer is the losing condition.
game_hooks: Boss sketch: LARGE size class. The lidded eye opens on a visible wind-up — a crew of four lid-bearers (destructible adds) hauls it open by the polished handle; the beam annihilates a lane (burn + dissolution affinities; his eye gained its venom from the fumes of his father's druids' brewing — disease_poison flavor). Discoverable win condition, never a damage race: stagger the lid crew to force a slow open, then strike THROUGH the opening eye at the wind-up moment — Lugh's sling-stone drove the eye out the back of his head so it blasted his own army; reflecting/redirecting the beam onto his adds is the second discoverable. Any other kill route fails, as prophecy said only his grandson could end him.
rating_notes: Influence 1 (Fomorian adversary, never worshipped); recognition 3 — 'Balor of the Evil Eye' circulates in games/anime at mythology-curious tier.

symbols: comb, razor, and shears between his ears

temperament: A deposed king who answers every summons to the show floor by wrecking it — the house hunts him for his grooming kit, never his head.
game_hooks: Boss sketch: HUGE size class, a king cursed into a boar (he can speak — use it). Condition affinities: poison (venomous bristles aura), crushed (trample charges), bleeding (tusk rake), exhausted (the endless cross-country chase drains pursuers). Seven piglet adds defend him to the death. THE objective-boss template for GPT: the win is STEALING the comb, razor, and shears from between his ears — three grab-windows during river-crossing stagger phases (Arthur's men took razor and shears at the Severn, the comb only in Cornwall) — he cannot be slain, only driven into the sea. Mythic-tier rarity: a collector's-board event boss.
rating_notes: Influence 1, recognition 1 (specialists only — Zababa anchor): honest mythic-tier collector content, deliberately not padded upward.

symbols: cobbler's hammer and a single unfinished shoe

temperament: House dealer with one unfinished shoe: every contract honest to the letter, and every letter a trapdoor.
game_hooks: Census luck_gambling flag — casino-native folk being. Dealer NPC: contract minigames resolved by letter-exact wording; the gold-crock wager (look away from him for one frame and the pot is forfeit — 'The Field of Boliauns' ends with a field of identical markers); wish events rigged to backfire unless phrased within N words (sim-checkable constraint). Art-direction deep cut: older sources dress him in RED — green is modern; a red-jacket skin is the collector variant. IP note: the folk figure is traditional/greenlit; avoid specific modern trade designs (cereal mascot, horror-film design).
rating_notes: Folk class, no worship = influence 1 (Baba Yaga anchor); global household image — carries the tradition's recognition ceiling per census = recognition 5.

temperament: The house herald: she has keened every elimination since the first season — always one round early, always just off-camera.
game_hooks: House herald (messenger used in the general herald sense, NOT the §3.3 corporate carve-out sense): her keen sounds tableside the round BEFORE an elimination becomes locked-in — a dramatic-irony broadcast tool the audience hears and the contestant doesn't. She never kills; she only announces (wrath 1, fidelity 5 — bound to specific families for generations). Clan-tag mechanic seed: she keens only for contestants with declared lineage tags (the Ó/Mac families of tradition). Event: the dropped silver comb — pick it up and you carry a death-omen debuff until it is returned to her windowsill.
rating_notes: Influence 1 (folk spirit, no cult); a global English loanword but the figure itself sits at Baba Yaga tier = recognition 4.

symbols: flat-topped imperial crown with twelve bead strings

temperament: Chairman of the celestial civil service — runs the apocalypse like a quarterly audit, never gambles personally, and files every miracle in triplicate.

favor_conditions: clear a floor with zero arena-rule infractions logged by the announcer
complete every posted side-objective on the run card before engaging the floor boss
publicly report your own rule violation before the broadcast catches it
expose another contestant's cheating on camera instead of profiting from it
taboos: falsely claim another god's blessing or title on camera
skip the table's opening rites — enter the run without saluting the house
defy a posted arena rule even when it would be profitable
patron_block.blessing_style: Edicts and promotions — boons arrive as stamped celestial writs: door clearances, rank passes, weather scheduled in your favor, and once per run an imperial pardon that voids a single penalty.

game_hooks: The house auditor: arena events where the celestial bureaucracy inspects the table mid-run, handing out buffs/debuffs per rule compliance. The Great Race is a ready-made VIP game format — twelve qualifying slots, one river crossing, alliances and betrayals scored live. His court besieged by Wukong seeds a 'palace under riot' boss arena. Pantheon caricature: the Chinese gods play the table as a vast government office — org charts, chops, performance reviews for deities.
rating_notes: Living Chinese-folk-religion supreme deity: rubric §3.1 anchors 'Chinese folk religion deities' at influence 4 (Amaterasu-comparable); the tradition's ceiling-5 is reserved for Caishen's larger active cult. Recognition 3 per census (mythology-curious tier, Väinämöinen-comparable).

temperament: The casino's market-maker — a New-Year rainmaker in a gold-trimmed suit who loves a bold position, remembers every ledger line, and will sell your contract the moment a better bid lands.

favor_conditions: open the run by wagering or offering your own currency at the table shrine
end the run with more currency than you entered with, after all fees
convert a boss drop into currency at the shop instead of equipping it
return another contestant's dropped loot bag intact on camera
taboos: be caught broke — reach zero currency on camera
wantonly destroy loot or currency
default on a debt owed to another contestant
patron_block.blessing_style: Compounding returns — small boons that multiply if reinvested rather than spent, with jackpot windfalls timed to festival broadcasts; his blessings always arrive as credit first.

game_hooks: Casino nobility (luck_gambling census flag): the god the whole floor already tips. Event seed: 'Fifth-Day Opening' — a market-floor level where every kill pays out in volatile scrip. Forsaken lens: his Zhao Gongming myth is dying on a losing bet — he backed the doomed Shang, was killed by effigy sorcery, and re-entered the market post-mortem via the Investiture — so he hosts Forsaken tables for debtors, offering resurrection-as-refinancing.
rating_notes: Most actively worshipped figure of a living major folk religion (well over 100M at Lunar New Year per Wave-1 census sources) = influence 5, Ganesha-comparable; recognition 3 — the practice is world-famous, the persona itself is mythology-curious tier in the West.

epithets_traditional: Correct God of Blessing and Virtue

temperament: Branch manager of the smallest branch of the celestial bank — knows every regular by name, comps modestly, always pays out on time, and is quietly one of the richest books in the room.

favor_conditions: salute the local shrine tile at the entrance of every floor
protect a non-combatant NPC through an entire fight
share consumables with a teammate who is below half supplies
clear the low-level pest enemies other contestants skip past
taboos: desecrate a shrine, grave, or field tile
mock a minor god's blessing on camera
patron_block.blessing_style: Small, constant, local — a heal at the doorstep, a discount at the floor shop, a lucky number whispered on his feast day; never flashy, never late.

game_hooks: luck_gambling census flag verified: he is the god Taiwanese lottery players petition first, and on his feast day joss-paper number tips appear at his shrines — in-game his shrines dispense one 'lucky number' intel hint per floor to the respectful. The VVIP paradox IS the story: an influence-4 bankroll inside a rare-board body — the penny-slots grandfather secretly seated at the Forsaken rail. Event seed: 'Feast Day' — every small shrine on the map pays out simultaneously.
rating_notes: Ubiquitous everyday shrine practice across the Chinese world = influence 4 (rubric's Chinese-folk-deity anchor, Amaterasu-comparable scale); recognition 2 per census (encyclopedia-depth outside Asia). Zhang Fude origin story is unverifiable folk legend, not a historical person — D-4-clean, same class as Zhong Kui's legendary backstory.

epithets_traditional: Golden Mother of the Jade Pool

temperament: The casino's oldest private-club hostess — a wild plague-queen of the mountains rebranded as the immaculate VVIP madam whose banquet invitations are the only guest list that matters, and whose orchard matures on a millennial vintage.

favor_conditions: finish the run with a legendary-grade consumable still unspent
reach the map's highest point before engaging the floor boss
cure another contestant's poison or infection at cost to yourself
pacify or tame a beast enemy instead of killing it
taboos: steal from a vault or orchard — loot flagged as another god's property
gatecrash a VIP event uninvited
patron_block.blessing_style: Invitations and elixirs — boons arrive as banquet gifts that grow more potent the longer they stay unspent; her highest favor is an invitation, and a refused invitation is never offered twice.

game_hooks: Item seed: Peaches of Immortality (census artifact folded here for slots) — jackpot-rare consumable that ripens across run count. Her archaic Shanhaijing form (tiger teeth, leopard tail, mistress of sky-calamities and the five destructive forces) is a boss skin: the hostess reverting to plague-queen when the banquet is violated — a disease_poison arena. Dual-aspect patron: healer to invited guests, pestilence to gatecrashers.
rating_notes: Living sectarian worship — Taiwan's Cihui Tang network (900+ branch halls venerating her as Yaochi Jinmu) plus broader folk cult = influence 4, Amaterasu-comparable; recognition 3 per census.

temperament: Maintenance director of reality itself — patches the sky on a contractor's schedule, matchmakes the staff, and if you graffiti her lobby she will foreclose your entire dynasty.

favor_conditions: repair or rebuild a broken arena structure mid-run
take a hit meant for a weaker teammate
win a fight using a crafted, not looted, weapon
recruit or reconcile two feuding contestants into one party
taboos: deface or mock her image or shrine on camera — the King Zhou clause
flee a floor while leaving its hazard spreading toward others
patron_block.blessing_style: Restoration — she mends what is broken: splinted limbs, rejoined gear, a five-colored patch sealing the party's worst structural hole; her vengeance is subcontracted and patient.

game_hooks: World-myth reenactment: 'mend the broken sky' = repair objectives completed under bombardment. Her Fengshen Yanyi vengeance — sending the fox spirit to rot a blasphemer's dynasty from inside — is the template for house-directed NPC saboteurs: she doesn't smite, she staffs. Kin ties (Fuxi) and the Gonggong flood-era repair job await later extraction batches.
rating_notes: Active regional cult (Shexian Wahuang Gong temple fair) = influence 3, continuous-folk-practice tier — a notch below the universal functional gods; recognition 3 per census.

symbols: courtroom dais of the Fifth Court

temperament: Head of collections and compliance — a magistrate who has processed every soul that ever lost at this casino, and is still furious about the one monkey who audited HIM.

favor_conditions: execute a condemned, marked target exactly as sentenced — no more, no less
state the charge on camera before delivering a killing blow to a beaten opponent
keep a personal kill ledger that matches the broadcast tally at run's end
taboos: kill an unmarked innocent NPC
use a revive or death-cheat without his sign-off
lie under oath on camera
patron_block.blessing_style: Stays of execution — his boons are time: death saves granted on bureaucratic technicalities, extended timers, and a glimpse of an enemy's remaining lifespan in the ledger.

game_hooks: Table boss: his Fifth Court is an arena — a courtroom raid where the win condition is procedural, not damage: steal or amend your own page of the ledger, Wukong-style. The ledger raid is the canonical 'cheat death on camera' reenactment. Runs the casino's collections desk with Hei Bai Wuchang as his repo team; Meng Po staffs his exit ramp.
rating_notes: The underworld courts anchor living Chinese funerary practice and Ghost Festival observance = influence 4 (Chinese-folk-deity anchor); recognition 3 — King Yama is mythology-curious tier globally. Syncretic group 'yama' declared for Wave-4 dedup against Hindu/Buddhist/Japanese identities.

temperament: The casino's bounty-hunting bar-exam ghost — aced the test, lost the job to a beauty clause, died in protest on the palace steps, and now eats the cheaters the house pretends not to see.

favor_conditions: kill a disguised or hidden enemy before it strikes
win a fight while under an active condition (bleeding, exhausted, or worse)
answer an arena riddle or exam correctly on the first try
avenge a wronged NPC or contestant on camera
taboos: accept a bribe to spare a demon or a cheater
attack an ugly-but-innocent NPC on appearance alone
patron_block.blessing_style: The demon register — he marks hidden enemies on your map and lends his sword arm in dreams; his boons hit harder the uglier the odds and the uglier the bearer.

game_hooks: Forsaken lens (census flag): his apotheosis is a self-unmaking — he destroyed himself in protest at a rigged system and was posthumously hired by that same system; as forsaken_host he runs tables for contestants the house has cheated, rigged AGAINST the house. Event seed: 'Zhong Kui Marries Off His Sister' — a night-parade escort level where the wedding procession is staffed by his tamed ghosts. Enemy-design seed: ghost retinue as recruitable ex-enemies.
rating_notes: Continuous talismanic and exorcistic folk practice (New Year door prints, Duanwu exorcism, nuo performance) = influence 3; recognition 3 per census. His failed-examinee backstory is legend, not a verified historical person — census D-4 screening kept him in.

temperament: The table's youngest high-roller — a lotus-bodied child marshal on flaming wheels who once settled a debt with his own dismembered body, and has considered every wager cheap since.

favor_conditions: engage the floor boss before any other contestant does
defeat an enemy of a higher tier than you in single combat
spend your own HP or a body part to secure a win (self-stake)
leave the run owing nothing — repay every debt and favor before the exit
taboos: harm a child NPC
kneel to extortion — pay off a shakedown instead of fighting it
patron_block.blessing_style: Escalation hardware — wind-fire wheels: stacking speed and fire boons that grow the longer you refuse to retreat; his rarest gift is the lotus rebuild, a one-time full-body reconstruction.

game_hooks: Forsaken lens (census flag): the tradition's cleanest self-unmaking myth — he carved up his own flesh and returned it to his parents to settle a god's grievance, then was rebuilt from lotus root; as forsaken_host he offers exactly that deal: your loss erased, your body rebuilt, your debt cleared, consent not included. Boss-turned-ally pattern from JTTW: house enforcer first, patron later. Techno-Nezha is a diegetic gift — his table plays EDM. Design caution: the current animated-film franchise designs are protected IP; the deity and the Ming corpus are not.
rating_notes: Major living cult — Zhongtan Yuanshuai / Santaizi temples across Taiwan plus Techno-Nezha performance culture = influence 4; recognition 4 (headlines China's biggest animated films, games staple) — Baba Yaga-comparable recognition anchor. ip public_domain_literary per census: defining myths are the Ming novels.

epithets_traditional: He Who Was Punished by Heaven

temperament: The casino's cautionary floor show — decapitated by management millennia ago and still on the floor every night, dancing his war dance with axe and shield, betting a head he no longer has.

favor_conditions: keep fighting after losing a body part, and win
refuse a surrender or ransom offer from a stronger enemy
land a killing blow while blinded or with a disabled sensory ability
taboos: surrender once a fight is joined
buy your way out of a fight you could still swing
patron_block.blessing_style: The headless drum — boons that trigger only at death's door: surging power while maimed, blinded, or past saving; nothing at full health, everything at the brink.

game_hooks: THE Forsaken mascot (census flag): a god who already lost everything to Heaven — head, war, crown — and kept fighting; his all-in has been running for four thousand years. Mythic-adjacent rare-board patron: a collector event whenever he bids. Enemy seed: headless duelist mini-boss whose 'weak point' head is buried elsewhere in the arena — discoverable win: strike the burial mound at Changyang Mountain, not the tireless body. His foe (the Yellow Emperor) awaits a later batch.
rating_notes: No attested worship ever — Shanhaijing mythography plus Tao Qian's poem only = influence 1, Zababa-comparable floor; recognition 2 per census. The honest floor entity the canon's rare-board collectors exist for.

temperament: The greatest contestant the tables ever produced — a stone-born nobody who ate the buffet, torched the VIP lounge, erased his own name from the collections ledger, and lost exactly one bet: to the only palm big enough to hold him.
game_hooks: The mortal-to-god pipeline made flesh — canon's contestant_legend archetype: he won so hard the casino had to hire him (his Great Sage cult means he now sits at the table he once flipped). Forsaken lens (census flag): the Buddha's-palm wager is the house legend — the one recorded all-in he LOST (stake: the throne of Heaven; payout: 500 years under a mountain), the story every dealer tells overconfident champions. Epithet-template gold: each feat (ledger raid, peach theft, 72 transformations, havoc in heaven) is a myth-reenactment epithet. Item seed: Ruyi Jingu Bang — the census artifact, folded here for slots (size-shifting eight-ton needle). Design caution: Black Myth: Wukong and Dragon Ball designs are protected; the JTTW corpus is not.
rating_notes: Recognition 5, Zeus-anchor comparable (global household icon). Influence 3: real continuous worship as Qitian Dasheng (temples in Fujian, Taiwan, Singapore, SE Asia) — the Väinämöinen calibration lesson inverted: literary fame PLUS a living cult lifts him off the influence floor.

temperament: The house's favorite tragedy — the marksman who saved the whole table from management's blazing sons and was billed for the damages: divinity revoked, wife gone to the moon, prize elixir drunk by someone else.
game_hooks: Forsaken lens (census flag): unmade BY his greatest victory — Di Jun stripped his immortality for killing the nine sun-sons, the archetype of a champion punished for winning too well. Legendary NPC contestant: the grizzled veteran who beat the impossible game and got fined for it. His trial is a ready-made precision minigame — ten suns, nine targets, and the hidden mercy rule (one must live) as the discoverable win condition. Chang'e and Di Jun await later batches.
rating_notes: No surviving worship, classical myth only = influence 1, Väinämöinen-comparable; recognition 3 — the Mid-Autumn backstory known to every Chinese schoolchild and mythology-curious audiences globally.

temperament: The casino vault made animal — everything goes in, nothing ever comes out, which is why every gambler on the floor rubs its statue on the way to the tables.
game_hooks: Enemy sketch (luck_gambling census flag) — vault-guardian mini-boss, size class M (lion-sized winged quadruped). Condition affinities: crushed (pounce and pin), exhausted (its hoard-room miser aura drains stamina from laden contestants). Discoverable win condition, never a damage race: it compulsively lunges to devour any thrown valuables and CANNOT expel them — feed it enough heavy loot and it bloats immobile, wings useless, belly exposed; the player too greedy to spend treasure literally cannot win. Drop seed: Pixiu charm — currency gains up, currency spending locked (the curse IS the blessing).
rating_notes: Talisman beast with live feng-shui practice but no worship = influence 1 (folk-class floor, Baba Yaga-consistent); recognition 2 per census — feng-shui famous in Asia, encyclopedia-depth beyond.

temperament: The casino's con artist in residence — three faces, one skeleton, zero refunds; her best disguise is the audience's sympathy.
game_hooks: Boss sketch — size class S (human-sized shapeshifter), three-phase social boss: appears as three sequential innocent NPCs (girl bringing food, then her grieving mother, then a frail elder). Condition affinities: poison (tainted offerings), dissolution (illusion assault on the mind). Discoverable win condition: expose the skeleton beneath BEFORE striking — true-sight consumable, camera-lens mechanic, or pattern deduction; killing an unexposed disguise reads as murdering a civilian on broadcast, crashing audience score and turning the party against you (the Tang Sanzang banishment beat as a mechanic). A boss that weaponizes the reality-TV frame itself.
rating_notes: Single-episode literary demon (JTTW ch. 27) = influence 1; recognition 2 per census — famous in the Sinosphere via the 'three strikes' episode, encyclopedia-depth elsewhere.

symbols: tall white hat inscribed 'fortune at first sight'

temperament: The casino's repo team and its most-petitioned tipsters — one drowned keeping an appointment, one hanged himself for missing it, and now they collect debts by day and leak winning numbers to the respectful by night.
game_hooks: Event/NPC pair (luck_gambling census flag, verified: worshipped as fortune deities who grant riches and winning lottery numbers): they appear when a contestant's timer or contract expires — bribable with joss offerings for one number-tip (a map coordinate, an enemy's exact HP, a drop rate), utterly incorruptible on the collection itself. Their origin — dying rather than break a bridge appointment — makes them the justice_oaths enforcement duo on every deal sheet: when a taboo triggers repossession, these two arrive, unfailingly polite. Strong escort-mission antagonists.
rating_notes: Active temple cult (City God temples, Taiwan's Qiye/Baye processions, SE Asian Tua Di Ya Pek veneration) = influence 3, continuous folk practice; recognition 2 per census.

epithets_traditional: Lady of the Bridge of Helplessness

temperament: The house always clears the table — she staffs the exit ramp of every ruined run, ladling out the broth that makes losers forget the odds and come back to play again.
game_hooks: The diegetic run-reset: between-run checkpoint NPC at the Bridge of Helplessness — contestants drink, the dungeon re-rolls, and no NPC remembers you (an in-world explanation for roguelike resets). Meta-progression hook: palming the bowl preserves one memory fragment across runs — carry-over with a cost, because the house notices an undrunk bowl. Boss-mechanic seed: memory-wipe zones that scramble learned boss patterns (madness_dream).
rating_notes: Universal afterlife belief but near-zero direct cult of her own = influence 2 (strong continuity without organized worship — judgment call argued in the dossier); recognition 2 per census.

epithets_traditional: Reckoner of Times and of Seasons

fully map a floor — every room revealed — before entering the boss chamber

destroy a book, scroll, tablet, or lore object
kill an ibis- or baboon-form creature

game_hooks: Roster patron AND the casino's notary — inter-pantheon contracts pass his desk (messenger role is intra-casino clerkship, not the Abrahamic corporate carve-out). Myth-recreation seed: the moonlight wager as a mid-season side-pot — contestants stake earned hype in a senet-style bracket, the pot paid out as extra 'days' (turns). His referee record in the 80-year Contendings makes him the canonical adjudicator NPC for disputed table rulings.
rating_notes: Influence 2 = Zeus anchor (extinct organized worship, strong cultural continuity, Egyptian core figure); recognition 4 = games/comics tier per census (SMITE, Moon Knight, occult afterlife via Hermes Trismegistus), below household-name Anubis at 5.

symbols: crook and flail over a mummiform sheath

temperament: The heir who pawned his own shine at the tables and has been paying it back one sliver a month ever since — charming, punctual, and always good for one more hand.

favor_conditions: win an encounter while a night or moon arena modifier is active
cure or stabilize a teammate's condition mid-combat
beat the floor's par time (the reckoner of lifespans rewards time in hand)
win a fight you entered with a lethal body part at critical HP
taboos: refuse to treat a wounded teammate when you hold the means
renege on a wager or side-bet declared on camera
patron_block.blessing_style: Boons wax and wane on a lunar cycle — near-double multipliers at his full phase, slivers at the new moon; he lends light and collects it back with interest.

game_hooks: Forsaken lens: the pantheon's one god with an attested divine-asset LOSS at a game — the moon's waning is his standing debt. Risk 5 makes him an eager Forsaken host: he runs moonlight-stake side pots where gods pawn attributes. Arena seed: a lunar-phase arena whose light level is literally his favor meter. Buried menace: his oldest form is the Cannibal Hymn's divine butcher — a berserk phase if a contestant defaults on him. IP caution: Marvel's 'Khonshu' design (Moon Knight) is protected; the god is traditional.
rating_notes: Influence 2 — core Theban-triad member, Zeus anchor tier; recognition 3 mythology-curious (census kept 3: Moon Knight raised the profile of 'Khonshu', a distinct protected design, not this god).

temperament: The firm's disgraced ex-heir — lost the succession suit after eighty years of litigation, got his name struck from the letterhead, and still clocks in nightly for the one security shift nobody else can work.

favor_conditions: defeat an enemy by destroying arena terrain or field objects rather than direct damage
kill a serpent-class enemy (he has standing on serpents)

taboos: refuse a duel or challenge issued to you on camera
spare a beaten opponent when the crowd calls for a finish
patron_block.blessing_style: Storms — arena-warping surges that hurt everyone but you slightly less; raw-strength multipliers that peak when you fight alone and booed.

game_hooks: Textbook Forsaken candidate: lost the 80-year succession suit, then lost his entire cult (demonized after Dynasty XX; Late Period execration rituals targeted him; worship survived only in remote oases) — a god rebuilding standing from nothing, prime all-in material. Leverage: he works the casino's nightly Apep-containment shift, the one job only he can do. Myth-recreation seed: the CHEST — he invented the rigged party game (a perfectly fitted box, promised to whoever fits it); runs beautifully as a mimic-sarcophagus event. His boons arrive as literal sandstorms.
rating_notes: Influence 2 — Egyptian core figure (Zeus anchor) despite the ancient demonization; recognition 4 per census (staple villain across games/film/TV).

temperament: Chairman emeritus, deceased — lost everything at a rigged party game once, and now owns the floor every loser lands on; he runs the Forsaken table because he's the only host who has been on both sides of the stake.

favor_conditions: return from a downed state and finish the run standing
recover every fallen teammate's body to the extraction point
reassemble a scattered objective — collect all pieces of a split relic in one run
stabilize or revive a teammate and end the run with them alive
taboos: abandon a dead teammate's body in the dungeon
finish an opponent who has surrendered on camera
eat fish rations on camera (cult taboo — Plutarch, Isis and Osiris 18)
patron_block.blessing_style: Slow, vegetal, posthumous — regeneration-over-time riders and one true resurrection per season, invoiced against future winnings.

game_hooks: The credible master of the Forsaken table: the one god already unmade once (sealed in the chest, dismembered into 14, reassembled) who returned as king of a different domain — existence-stake players trust HIS deal. Faction head of the death-paperwork bloc (Anubis, Thoth, Ammit, Medjed). Per BD 175 he is also one of only two beings scheduled to survive Atum's final all-in — the house's designated last creditor.
rating_notes: Influence 2 = Zeus anchor (extinct organized worship, massive cultural continuity); recognition 4 per census — mythology-literate famous, a step below household-name Anubis.

He Who Is in the Place of Embalming (Imiut)

decline a feast, drink, or festival event offered by the table
patron_block.blessing_style: Fever — stacked damage with burn/infected riders that cook you too (exhaustion debt after every surge); appease her at festivals or the burning doesn't stop.

game_hooks: Table-boss seed straight from her myth: the Red Flood arena — the field slowly floods with 'blood' and the party wins by getting her to DRINK it (placing red-beer lures at choke points); damaging her only accelerates the massacre timer. Discoverable win = appeasement, never DPS (the gods themselves couldn't out-fight her; they out-catered her). Healer inversion: her priesthood were physicians, so a pacified Sekhmet flips to the party's strongest healing buff.
rating_notes: Influence 2 — core figure of the extinct cult (Zeus anchor); recognition 3 per census — mythology-curious tier, not yet games-household.

mock a smaller or weaker contestant on camera
patron_block.blessing_style: Loud, ugly, lucky — once per floor the bad roll quietly comes up your way, and his protection is always thickest over the party's weakest member.

game_hooks: The people's god at the people's tables: 'Bes Night' home-table modifier — every contestant's single worst roll per floor is invisibly rerolled. Event NPC inversion: a tiny guardian who cannot be beaten by force, only by making the crowd laugh (charm/camera check). His attested myth role — dancing the raging lioness goddess home at Philae — seeds a pacify-the-boss support mechanic that pairs him directly with Sekhmet's Red Flood fight.
rating_notes: Influence 2 — never a state god but millennium-spanning household worship across all classes (rubric: extinct organized worship, strong continuity); recognition 2 encyclopedia-depth per census. Luck flag is apotropaic ('wards off bad luck' — World History Encyclopedia), not a dice myth — noted honestly.

temperament: The house odds-setter — assigned to your account at birth, present at its closing, and never once surprised by a result.

favor_conditions: resolve a Forced Action exactly as rolled — no mitigation, no reroll — and still win the encounter
declare the run's outcome on camera before it starts and hit it exactly

quit a run before its allotted end (mid-run abandonment)

game_hooks: Casino nobility per §4 (luck_gambling): fate as a god — born beside each mortal, allotting lifespan and fortune, standing at the final weighing (Papyrus of Ani vignette). NPC: the intake odds-setter who reads each contestant's file and posts 'Shai's Line' — a house fate-line per run that pays out everyone who bet it if matched exactly. The delicious irony is a luck god with risk_appetite 2: he never gambles because nothing is a risk to him; his table appearances are settlements. Mythic-tier collector god — rare boards, cult favorite when he bids.
rating_notes: Zababa anchor exactly: minor personification with modest cult, extinct, specialists only = influence 1, recognition 1 — the honest floor, not padded (mythic tier is a canon feature).

kill a cobra- or serpent-class creature that has not attacked you

Condition affinities: suffocation (constriction and swallowing), poison (fangs), dissolution (the entrancing stare — psychic). Discoverable win condition: he CANNOT be killed by damage — he reforms nightly by nature; damage only opens ritual windows. The party must discover and perform the four execration acts of the Book of Overthrowing Apep (spear, bind, knife, fire) in order, within phase windows, while keeping a 'barque' escort objective alive until dawn. Forsaken lens (census flag): the being that is unmade daily and returns is the dark mirror of every existence stake — casino canon slot: the escrow-beast beneath the Forsaken table, into which erased gods are fed.
rating_notes: Influence 1 — never worshipped, only execrated (worship-based bankroll = zero); recognition 3 per census (Stargate/SMITE/Assassin's Creed keep 'Apophis' in genre circulation).

game_hooks: Boss/enforcer sketch — size: large. Condition affinities: crushed (crocodile jaws), bleeding (lion foreclaws). Discoverable win condition: she cannot be fought, only starved — she may ONLY attack the party member with the heaviest 'sin-weight' (taboo breaches accrued this run); the fight is a courtroom: rebalance the scales via confession/weight-transfer interactions at the scale altar until no one is lawfully devourable, and she withdraws (she cannot devour the justified). Damage only provokes. Casino role: the house's repo-agent, dispatched when a voided patron contract is called in — her bite is the 'second death' (BD 125), the same erasure a lost Forsaken stake buys.
rating_notes: Influence 1 — no cult ever (a judicial function, not a goddess); recognition 3 per census, boosted by her turn as Moon Knight's antagonist (that design is Marvel IP; the composite beast is traditional).

His entire attestation is two lines of Book of the Dead 17 ('who smites with his eye, yet is unseen', House of Osiris) — the mystery IS the content. Enemy variant: an untargetable minion that must be revealed by light/lantern fields before it can be hit at all. Modern-life attribution imported diegetically: the casino gift shop's best-selling plush (his real Japanese meme career post-2012 Greenfield Papyrus exhibition).

game_hooks: Item seed — legendary cursed relic. Powers (per Setne I's two spells): read the floor — all enemy intents and the floor bestiary revealed, hidden win conditions visible ('understand the speech of animals'; 'see the gods'). Curse: while carried, 'the audit' escalates (god-sent hazards each floor); the previous owner's ghost challenges the carrier to a board game with sinking stakes — each lost game hammers the carrier into the floor, draining one body part's HP tier (the Setne tomb scene, verbatim as an in-run event). Master it by winning the ghost game, or return it to its tomb for a payout. Acquisition heist: the nested-chest vault guarded by a deathless serpent that reknits unless both halves are kept apart.

Natural operator of an underworld side-table where eliminated contestants keep playing for their exit. Time-manipulation arenas (par-time floors, ripening windows); devour-the-drop mechanic; his freed-prisoner taboo is the mythically exact one — freed prisoners (the Cyclopes) are what armed his overthrow.

A correct answer triggers her mythologically exact self-destruction; a wrong answer forfeits a party member's turn to her grab. FORSAKEN PATTERN NOTE: she staked her own existence on every riddle asked and paid up instantly on her first loss — the beast-tier mirror of the any-god-all-in rule; reality-TV quiz-show framing is free (§4 canon: VIP tables run pop-culture formats).
