symbols: the chains that once held Death

temperament: Beat the house twice on technicalities, so the house made him the technicality — eternity's live demo of what happens to card-counters, still grinning like he knows a third loophole.
game_hooks: EVENT/NPC HOOKS: the eternal gambler in the underworld side-lounge — an NPC who sells death-cheat intel and never mentions the price he paid. Boulder event: a push-to-summit objective that fully resets on any party death (the audience loves it). ARTIFACT SEED via his myth: the Chains of Thanatos — one-use item that 'jails Death' for one Clock (nobody on the table can die), directly violating Hades' death-cheat taboo for delicious patron-conflict. His two successful death-cheats make him the mascot of every contestant playing a rigged table anyway.
rating_notes: Recognition 4 — 'Sisyphean' is common speech and Camus made him a modern icon; influence 1 — legendary king with punished-mortal status, no cult (folk-class practical ceiling per Baba Yaga anchor).

temperament: Flat rate, exact change, no comps, no credit — the only service in the cosmos that has never once missed a delivery.
game_hooks: NPC/EVENT HOOKS: the toll-booth dealer at floor transitions — exact-change mechanic (carry a reserved obol or wait out a full Clock at the gate; the coin slot takes ONE coin, never more, never less). Ferry as diegetic fast-travel between cleared districts. Two canonical exploits, both costly: music moves him (Orpheus) and force works exactly once and gets him a year in chains (Heracles) — so the house now fines the CONTESTANT for it. Loot flavor: 'Charon's Obol' as a follower-relic revival token that Hades-pact contestants must refuse.
rating_notes: Recognition 3 — mythology-curious tier (the Hades game and 'Charon's obol' idiom carry him); influence 1 — a psychopomp daimon receiving funeral custom (the obol), not worship.

temperament: The floor's beloved first-bet concierge — every new player's chips pass under his trunk for luck before touching a table, and the house never opens a season without him.

favor_conditions: perform an offering/salute action at the run's first threshold before any combat
clear a blocking obstacle (barricade, rubble, locked route) instead of routing around it
learn a boss's win condition before dealing it any damage
sacrifice a piece of your own equipped gear to complete an objective (the broken-tusk clause)
taboos: mock a fallen contestant's failure on camera (the moon clause)
skip an available threshold offering at a run's start
let food supplies spoil or go to waste
patron_block.blessing_style: obstacles quietly resolve ahead of you — locked doors found open, traps pre-sprung; boons front-load run starts and the first attempt of anything

game_hooks: Run-start blessing economy: threshold offerings, first-attempt bonuses, pre-sprung traps. Moon-curse event: any contestant who mocks a fallen rival on camera catches the 'False Accusation' debuff, cleared by publicly tracing the Syamantaka jewel. Not a Forsaken candidate — the house's perennial favorite, never desperate.
rating_notes: FROZEN Wave-0 calibration entity — matches exactly: principal deity of a living major (>100M) = influence 5; globally recognized image short of Zeus-tier household name = recognition 4.
sources: Brahma Vaivarta Purana Ganapati Khanda (via verified index)

temperament: Ash-smeared whale who meditates through whole seasons, then bets everything — clothes included — on one hand; the house comps his suite and prays he stays asleep.

favor_conditions: voluntarily strip an equipped item or active buff before a boss and win without it
absorb a poison or burn condition meant for a teammate and survive the run (the halahala clause)
hold position without attacking for 3+ consecutive turns mid-combat, then win the encounter (the tapas clause)
finish a boss with a performance/flourish action on camera (the Tandava)
taboos: interrupt another contestant's rest or channeled action (the Kamadeva clause)
kill a non-hostile beast (Pashupati protects the wild)
boast about a victory on camera
patron_block.blessing_style: long silence, then everything at once — no drip-feed; favor accumulates unseen and detonates as one overwhelming third-eye intervention

game_hooks: Forsaken-host archetype (census forsaken_candidate; risk_appetite 5): the god who already lost everything at dice — down to his clothes (Skanda Purana) — and keeps returning to the table; hosts an all-or-nothing ash table. Third-eye = one-per-season patron ultimate that deletes a single encounter. Halahala event: a spreading poison pool one contestant must drink and hold (poison-condition endurance) to save the floor.
rating_notes: Principal deity of a living major (Shaivism, hundreds of millions) = influence 5 per Ganesha anchor; genuine global household name (Nataraja, 'the Destroyer') = recognition 5, Zeus-tier.
sources: Mahabharata Adi Parva, Astika (Ganguli tr., PD)

temperament: The only player on the floor with a lifetime winning record against the Destroyer — patient, immaculate, seated facing the door, and she brought her own banker.

favor_conditions: win a wager minigame or duel against an opponent with higher stats
retry a failed objective until it clears — 3+ attempts on the same objective, then success (the tapas clause)
shield a downed teammate until they recover
feed a starving NPC from your own supplies (the Annapurna clause)
taboos: abandon a party member mid-run
accept a rival patron's buy-out offer
refuse hospitality or food offered by an NPC
patron_block.blessing_style: steady maternal compounding — small persistent buffs that grow each consecutive run you keep faith with her; break the streak and the ledger resets

game_hooks: Casino royalty: the reigning dice champion — she beat Shiva with Lakshmi quietly staking her (Skanda Purana ch. 34) and decreed the Diwali gambling night. Diwali table modifier: all wagers prosper. Couples-table event reenacting the Akshakrida. Forsaken lens: census forsaken_candidate via the wager myths; risk 4 = an eager (never desperate) host. As her Durga battle-form she underwrites the Mahishasura boss table.
rating_notes: Principal goddess of the living major (pan-Indian worship, Shaktism) = influence 5 per Ganesha anchor; known mainly to mythology-curious audiences abroad = recognition 3 (census concurs).

symbols: gold coins pouring from an open palm

temperament: Owns the luckiest seat in the house and never sits in it twice — brag about her once and she is already gone, her sister moving into your chair.

favor_conditions: end the run with more currency than you entered with
enter the finale carrying no broken gear (she abides where things are kept well)
give a share of loot to a teammate or NPC during the run
place or light light-sources in dark map cells (the Diwali clause)
taboos: boast about winnings on camera (fortune flees the boastful)
idle through a full run phase (sloth invites her sister)
leave a debt unpaid at run's end
patron_block.blessing_style: compounding luck on loot rolls and shop odds while her conditions hold — and instant, silent departure the moment one breaks; her exit is the only warning

game_hooks: The deepest bankroll on the floor. Table aura: loot-luck multiplier while her conditions hold. Departure mechanic: a per-table boast meter — max it and she walks mid-run, hot-swapping her aura for Alakshmi's (paired-goddess rule). Her walk-out from Indra's heaven (Vishnu Purana I.9) is the myth template for patron abandonment; low fidelity is canonical (Chanchala, 'the fickle').
rating_notes: Principal deity invoked in virtually every Hindu household and business (Diwali) = influence 5 per Ganesha anchor; internationally recognizable but at Ganesha's visual tier, not Zeus's = recognition 4 (census concurs).

temperament: The slow auditor in iron-rimmed glasses — the one dealer no god can tip; he comps nothing, forgets nothing, and settles every account with interest, eventually.

favor_conditions: accept a run-start handicap (curse or debuff) offered by an event and clear the run without cleansing it (the sade-sati clause)
pay every toll, fee, or NPC debt in full at first demand
complete a full run using zero consumable items
finish a fight you started even after it turns unfavorable (no mid-combat retreat)
taboos: cheat or exploit a rigged advantage in any wager event
abandon an accepted quest or contract mid-run
patron_block.blessing_style: back-loaded justice — no boons early; every hardship endured is logged and paid out at run's end with interest, the longer the drought the bigger the settlement

game_hooks: The incorruptible house auditor: counter-NPC to Shakuni's rigged tables (invoke an audit to expose loaded dice — the one dealer who cannot be tipped, inverting the canon tipping rule for flavor). Sade-sati contract: accept a seven-phase handicap for a massive back-loaded payout. His gaze as a floor hazard: a moving line-of-sight beam that debuffs whatever it touches — it took an infant god's head off (Brahma Vaivarta Purana).
rating_notes: Dedicated living worship at scale (Shani Shingnapur, ubiquitous Saturday/navagraha observance — tens of millions) = influence 4 per Amaterasu anchor, below the >100M principal tier; encyclopedia-depth outside India = recognition 2.
sources: Brahma Vaivarta Purana Ganapati Khanda (via verified index)

temperament: The pot-bellied cage boss who has lent bankrolls to gods — one loan is still compounding at Tirupati — and whose terms outlive religions.

favor_conditions: end the run with more banked currency than you spent
repay any in-run loan or debt before its deadline, with the interest
discover a hidden treasure cache or vault room
end the run with every party member's loot intact — nothing lost, dropped, or stolen
taboos: default on a loan or stake you cannot cover
destroy or abandon loot that could have been carried out
wager the party's shared pot without consent
patron_block.blessing_style: lines of credit, not gifts — advance boons early in the run that must be repaid from winnings, at fair terms while you're good for them

game_hooks: The casino's banker and cage: in-run lines of credit with divine interest. The Venkateswara wedding-loan storyline — a supreme god in debt to him until the end of the age, with devotees paying the interest — is a ready-made season-long economy plot. Vault-heist dungeon theme: Alaka's treasuries with yaksha guards. Wave-4 note: same identity as Buddhist Vaisravana/Jambhala (syncretic_group declared for dedup).
rating_notes: Continuous folk practice (Dhanteras invocation with Lakshmi, temple presence) but little dedicated cult of his own = influence 3; encyclopedia-depth = recognition 2 (census concurs).
sources: Venkatachala Mahatmya tradition / Tirumala temple lore (verified via multiple 2024-25 accounts)

symbols: lime-and-chili ward (hung to keep her out)

temperament: Fortune's unlucky elder sister works the cold tables — nothing to her name, everything to prove, and she will go all-in on your losing streak.

favor_conditions: win an encounter while carrying an uncleansed curse
loot a fallen rival contestant's stash
enter the boss room with broken equipment and still win
provoke two NPC factions into fighting each other
taboos: cleanse a curse or purify a shrine mid-run
share food or supplies with the party (harmony repels her)
carry a purification or ward charm in inventory
patron_block.blessing_style: misfortune redirected — she doesn't give you luck, she takes yours to your enemies; boons land as critical failures on whoever stands against you

game_hooks: Forsaken-host natural (§5 amendment): a goddess acknowledged by a billion-strong religion only to be ritually expelled from it — the desperate, nothing-to-lose profile; staking her own existence is fully in-character. Paired mechanic with Lakshmi: one arrives when the other leaves. Strife events: a party 'quarrel meter' that powers her boons. Collector thrill: mythic-tier rarity on the boards per canon.
rating_notes: Judgment call: living tradition acknowledges her only apotropaically (Diwali expulsion, lime-chili wards) — continuous practice but zero positive worship-wealth = influence 2, not 3; specialists only = recognition 1.
sources: Padma Purana churning account + Sri Sukta khila (elder-sister reference), verified one level down

temperament: The legend the pit bosses whisper about: lost a kingdom to possessed dice, came back knowing the heart of every die on the floor.
game_hooks: Legendary NPC contestant: a past-season champion who diced away his realm under hostile possession and won it back in one all-in rematch. Teaches 'Aksha-Hridaya' (the Heart of Dice, learned from Rituparna for his horse-lore) as an unlockable perk: predict/reroll wager events, count hidden totals at a glance (the vibhitaka-tree feat). Epithet template: 'the Dice-Hearted' — lose everything mid-run and win it all back within the same season. Forsaken lens: census forsaken_candidate; his ruin was engineered, his comeback a single throw.
rating_notes: No worship; epic-literary fame inside a living tradition = influence 1 per Väinämöinen anchor; Nala-Damayanti is encyclopedia-depth classic = recognition 2 (census concurs).
sources: Mahabharata Vana Parva, Nalopakhyana (Ganguli tr., PD; sacred-texts.com/hin/m03/m03077.htm)

animals: dog (Dharma in disguise, at heaven's gate)

temperament: The straightest player in the room and the easiest mark — he cannot decline a challenge, and the house knows it.
game_hooks: Legend NPC: the honest whale every casino covets. Truth-tracker mechanic: his chariot rode above the ground until his first half-lie (Drona Parva) — a visible purity meter that drops on deception. Event chains: the unrefusable challenge (kshatriya code as compulsion), the Yaksha's riddle lake with brothers' lives staked on answers, refusing paradise without his dog. Epithet template: 'Never-Bluffs' — clear a run with zero deception actions. Forsaken lens: census forsaken_candidate — staked kingdom, brothers, wife, self; the cautionary all-in.
rating_notes: Epic hero within a living tradition, near-zero cultic presence = influence 1 per Väinämöinen anchor; encyclopedia-depth = recognition 2 (census concurs); 'living' recorded because the epic is actively sacred.
sources: Mahabharata Sabha Parva (dyuta), Vana Parva (Yaksha Prashna), Mahaprasthanika/Svargarohana Parva (Ganguli tr., PD)

temperament: The dealer you never want smiling at you — his dice answer to family loyalty, not physics, and he only plays hands he has already won.
game_hooks: The crooked dealer: runs rigged wager events where his dice never miss (Sabha Parva). Discoverable counter — you cannot outroll him: expose him via Shani's audit or Nala's dice-heart perk, refuse correctly, or change the game. Boss variant: 'The Uncle's Table.' Forsaken lens: census forsaken_candidate — a man who wagered a decade of patience on one revenge and cashed out a world war. Honesty flag: the 'dice carved from his father's bones' detail is late/folk tradition, not the critical epic (dossier).
rating_notes: One documented active temple (Malanada, Kerala — toddy and silk offerings, no Vedic puja) doesn't clear the folk-practice bar = influence 1, but justifies depiction_risk low over none; encyclopedia-depth = recognition 2 (census concurs).

temperament: Not a player — the house rot itself; he lives inside the dice and only steps out when you're already losing.
game_hooks: Boss sketch: medium possession-spirit (humanoid shadow). Condition affinities: exhausted (compulsion drains), dissolution (psychic corruption), poison (he entered Nala when ritual purity lapsed). Mechanic: he inhabits an item — a vibhitaka die seeded into party loot; while carried, a possessed member is periodically Forced-Action'd into bad wagers and misdirected attacks (the rulebook's Forced Action d6 is literally his voice). Discoverable win: damage is useless — he re-possesses; identify the vessel and expel him into a tree or container (as Nala expelled him into the vibhitaka tree), then cleanse. Forsaken lens: census forsaken_candidate + luck_gambling — he IS the embodied losing streak. Disambiguation: Sanskrit Kali (कलि), NOT the goddess Kālī.
rating_notes: No worship (the age's corruption personified) = influence 1, recognition 1 — the Zababa-anchor honest floor; his myth (Nalopakhyana) is nonetheless first-rank gambling material.
sources: Mahabharata Vana Parva, Nalopakhyana (Ganguli tr., PD)
Rig Veda 10.34 Gambler's Hymn (Griffith tr., PD) for the vibhitaka dice

temperament: The banned guest who still gets in — a severed head on a smoke-grey tab, drinking comped amrita he already paid for with his body.
game_hooks: Boss sketch: large — a colossal severed head trailing smoke, no body. Condition affinities: suffocation (swallowing), chilled (the eclipse shadow kills warmth and light), dissolution. Arena mechanic: periodic eclipse phases — he swallows the arena's light source; healing and light-buffs invert while it's swallowed. Discoverable win: he is ONLY a head — whatever he swallows passes out the open neck (that's why eclipses end); the kill window is the throat during a swallow: send a sun-charged object or teammate through and strike the stump from behind. Never a damage race — the head reforms. Floor-hazard variant: a daily 'Rahu kalam' window when all wagers sour (the real observance). Forsaken lens: census forsaken_candidate — already unmade once at the gods' own table; gate-crashed the biggest pot in history and paid with his body.
rating_notes: Living propitiatory practice at real scale (daily Rahu-kalam observance, Srikalahasti Rahu-Ketu kshetra with daily pujas) = influence 3 (continuous folk practice, fear-cult not principal worship); encyclopedia-depth = recognition 2 (census concurs).
sources: Mahabharata Adi Parva, Astika (Ganguli tr., PD)

temperament: A bull-market bully who bought invulnerability with one fatal exclusion clause and never read the fine print.
game_hooks: Boss sketch: huge shapeshifter cycling buffalo → lion → man → elephant → buffalo (the Devi Mahatmya's phase order). Condition affinities: crushed (trample), bleeding (gore), exhausted (attrition through endless phase-cycling). Discoverable win: his boon excludes death from any male god or man (Devi Mahatmya / Devi Bhagavata) — only a female contestant, or a party that forges the 'Assembled Goddess' buff by every member donating a weapon or power (Durga's own creation from the gods' pooled energies), can land the kill; his final phase is flatly immune otherwise, teaching the exclusion. Sensitivity: some Asur/Santal communities commemorate him in mourning — play him tragedy-grade, never mocking (dossier).
rating_notes: Slain adversary, not a worship object (marginal modern counter-commemoration noted, below the folk-practice bar) = influence 1; known via annual Durga Puja imagery = recognition 2 (census concurs); 'living' recorded for his active role in a living festival's iconography.
sources: Devi Mahatmya / Markandeya Purana (Pargiter tr. 1904, PD)

symbols: corpse hanging upside-down from a tree

temperament: The graveyard-shift riddle dealer: twenty-four hands, your life on each answer, and the tale is somehow always about you.
game_hooks: Event NPC / riddle dealer: the corpse that must be carried. Night-zone escort event: a vetala-inhabited corpse poses riddle-tales; answer correctly aloud = it flies back to the tree (restart, stakes raised); answer wrong when you know better = head-burst (massive dissolution penalty); genuine silence in ignorance = safe passage. Complete the full cycle and he reveals the quest-giver's betrayal, flipping an ambush — exactly how he saved Vikramaditya from the sorcerer. Hub role: runs a life-wagered riddle side-table on the graveyard shift. Dossier notes the living Konkan/Goa Betal gramadevata cult as a distinct, derived guardian figure (hence risk low, not none).
rating_notes: Literary spirit-class = influence 1; D&D-familiar encyclopedia-depth = recognition 2 (census concurs); depiction_risk upgraded none→low for the documented living Betal village cult (Goa/Sindhudurg), a derived but related figure.
sources: Vetala Panchavimshati / Kathasaritsagara bk. 12; Burton, Vikram and the Vampire (1870, PD, gutenberg.org/files/2400)
Shree Betal temple + Betal (Wikipedia, verified one level down)

animals: lion and bear (its fatal chain of custody)

temperament: A jackpot that audits its winner — eight bars of gold a day for the worthy, a burst artery and a manhunt for anyone else.
game_hooks: Item seed: legendary loot, the Sun's own jewel. While the holder is 'worthy' (zero taboo/dishonesty logs this run) it trickles gold — eight bars per floor (the Vishnu Purana's eight bharas a day). Any dishonest act flips it cursed: it broadcasts the holder's position to every treasure-hunting enemy on the floor and applies bleeding (its unworthy holders died to a lion and to ambush). It cannot be destroyed, only honestly given away — which clears the curse. Tie-in: the 'False Accusation' moon-sight debuff (Ganesha's curse) clears by publicly proving where the jewel went, as Krishna had to.
rating_notes: Artifact, no worship = influence 1; specialists only = recognition 1 (census concurs) — mythic-tier collector loot, exactly the canon's rare-board thrill.
sources: Vishnu Purana IV.13 (Wilson tr., PD); Bhagavata Purana X.56-57

animals: long-singing rooster of the Eternal Land

temperament: Chairwoman-emeritus of the eight-million-kami Takamagahara talent agency; one on-air outrage from her brother and she'll cancel every broadcast in existence — her staff keep a mirror, a comedian, and a strongman on retainer for the next blackout.

favor_conditions: Clear a dungeon floor engaging enemies only in lit tiles or after deploying a light source — never fight in darkness
Recover the crowd from a hype-meter floor to a cheer within the same fight (stage a comeback the audience laughs or roars at)
Keep every promise sworn on camera for an entire run (declared pledges tracked, zero breaches)
Lure a fortified boss out of its lair instead of storming in — make the enemy come to you
taboos: Defile a camp, shrine, or rest zone (leave corpses or filth where allies eat and sleep)
Break an oath sworn on camera
Abandon an ally alone in darkness
patron_block.blessing_style: Daylight banking: steady boons that compound while conduct stays clean — and a total blackout of favor (absence, not punishment) the moment the deal-sheet is dirtied; her light returns only after a genuine crowd-winning comeback.

game_hooks: The rock-cave myth is a casino-wide table event: the lights go out on every board at once and only spectacle brings them back — a hype-engine event where the arena boss must be lured out by audience reaction, not damage. As investor she is the flagship VVIP bankroll of the Shinto delegation; her deal sheet punishes dirty play with silent divestment rather than curses.
rating_notes: FROZEN Wave-0 calibration entity, matched exactly: living significant practice (Ise Grand Shrine; national shrine network) = influence 4 not 5 (below the >100M majors); games/anime staple (Okami, Naruto) = recognition 4; living gate recorded per §3.3 (data, not a gate, per v2 ruling).

temperament: The agency's scandal-ridden rock star on his eternal redemption tour — fined a thousand tables, shaved, and thrown out of the building once already, which is exactly why he'll go all-in at any table that will still seat him.

favor_conditions: Get a monster to consume a prepared bait, drink, or trap before engaging it (the eight-vats play)
Win a fight entered under a visible penalty or handicap the audience can see
Protect a designated NPC through an entire boss fight without them taking a hit
Declare a formal contest or bet against a stronger rival on camera — and win it
taboos: Refuse a challenge or wager that was formally declared to your face
Harm or abandon a charge you swore on camera to protect
Kill a weeping supplicant who asked for help
patron_block.blessing_style: All-in weather: boons arrive as storms — huge, messy, front-loaded windfalls (found weapons, monster-staggering gales) with collateral damage priced in; he tips biggest when you bet like him.

game_hooks: FORSAKEN LENS: the canonical fallen-and-clawed-back god — the Kojiki has him literally FINED ('a fine of a thousand tables'), stripped (beard cut, nails pulled), and expelled from heaven, and his ukehi vow with Amaterasu is a straight divine wager decided by producing children from exchanged tokens; risk_appetite 5 makes him an eager Forsaken host who stakes big because he has already survived losing everything. Event hook: exile-format shows (contestant stripped of gear mid-run, must rebuild from a serpent-infested floor).
rating_notes: Chief kami of the Yasaka/Gion, Tsushima, and Hikawa shrine networks with Japan's biggest festival (Gion Matsuri, running since the 869 plague rites) = influence 4 beside the Amaterasu anchor; census provisional recognition 4 (Naruto, countless games) — one notch under Zeus-5.

temperament: The graveyard-shift host who killed a celebrity chef over the tasting menu and has been contractually barred from daytime programming ever since; he and the chairwoman split the schedule so they never share a studio.

favor_conditions: Complete a full fight or floor in darkness or at night without using a light source
Announce a single-strike kill on camera and land it in exactly one action (he ended Ukemochi in one stroke)
Refuse offered food, drink, or loot from a defeated or suspect source for a whole run
taboos: Consume or loot provisions produced from a corpse's body (his original outrage)
Attack during a parley or feast you formally accepted
patron_block.blessing_style: Cold lunar scheduling: boons arrive precisely on a cycle (every Nth turn, every night phase), never early and never negotiable — miss the window and it simply passes.

game_hooks: The estrangement is diegetic scheduling drama: he and Amaterasu never attend the same table — night-shift boards run under his eye, and a contestant favored by both siblings must split the run into strict day/night phases. Event hook: a formal in-dungeon banquet where etiquette checks, not combat, decide the reward — with instant wrath escalation on a breach.
rating_notes: Thin actual cult (Tsukuyomi-no-miya at Ise, Gassan shrine) = influence 3, honestly below his influence-4 siblings; recognition 3 rides Naruto/Final Fantasy name-drops — the mythology-curious anchor tier (Väinämöinen band, but with real if small worship).

temperament: Founder's widow running the basement tables; she built the company campus with her own hands, died doing it, and now collects a thousand souls a day in alimony.

favor_conditions: Meet a declared kill quota before the floor timer ends (she counts a thousand a day)
Finish a fight while carrying the burn condition (she died of her burns and kept ruling)
Strike down a former ally or beloved NPC turned enemy without hesitating
Hold position in pitch darkness while something hunts you
taboos: Look at or expose what she has hidden (peek at her concealed intel, unmask her disguised agents)
Abandon a partner mid-run and flee alone (Izanagi's crime)
Seal an escape route behind you that a partner still needs
patron_block.blessing_style: The collections desk: her boons are debts called in from your enemies — rot, exhaustion, and attrition land on the far side of the table; her favor never heals you, it makes everyone else die faster. Payment due daily.

game_hooks: FORSAKEN LENS (census-flagged): the goddess who lost everything — life, family, face — and now rules from the bottom; the canonical nothing-left-to-lose Forsaken host, and the 1,000-a-day oath is a ready-made quota economy. Table-boss sketch: a Yomi arena — pitch-dark pursuit floor where LOOKING at the boss (inspect/scan actions) reveals the rot, triggers the Eight Thunders adds, and locks the exit; win condition is reaching Yomotsu Hirasaka and sealing the pass, not killing her — she cannot die again.
rating_notes: Continuous but localized veneration (Iya Shrine and Hibayama in Shimane, Hananoiwaya in Kumano — verified) = influence 3; recognition 3 via SMT/Persona-class game appearances — mythology-curious tier, consistent with the Väinämöinen/Zababa floor calibration.

epithets_traditional: Great God of the Five Grains

temperament: Head of franchising — thirty thousand branch locations, fox couriers on every floor, and a compounding-interest smile; the only executive whose portfolio has grown every single century.

favor_conditions: Tithe the first item of every loot pile at a camp altar before touching the rest
Feed or supply a beast companion or NPC before yourself at each rest stop
Reinvest winnings mid-run — spend earnings on upgrades instead of banking them
Repair or forge an item mid-run (the Kokaji patronage: Inari personally hammers alongside smiths)
taboos: Kill a fox or fox-like creature
Cheat a merchant or vendor NPC on camera
Leave a floor shrine defiled after finding it
patron_block.blessing_style: Franchise compounding: many small boons that multiply on streaks — every tithe returns with interest, delivered by fox couriers; miss a payment and the interest reverses.

game_hooks: LUCK FLAG (census): Japan's patron of getting rich — the casino's most natural investor-god, whose branch-shrine empire out-locations every other kami brand. Fox-courier dealers (kitsune floor staff) deliver boons physically as wrapped offerings; a contestant who unwraps them on camera feeds the hype engine. Loot hook: fox-forged weapons bear two signatures, the smith's and the god's (Kogitsune-maru pattern) — dual-maker items as a rarity class.
rating_notes: Most-enshrined kami in Japan (~30,000+ branch shrines, Fushimi Inari Taisha head — verified) = influence 4 (the rubric's kami anchor tier); torii-tunnel and fox imagery is a global games staple (Persona, Okami, countless) = recognition 4.

animals: mouse (his rescuer in the burning field)

temperament: The bought-out founder: signed the whole visible world over to the chairwoman's family, kept the grandest venue and the matchmaking desk — and once a year every god in the building attends HIS conference.

favor_conditions: Heal or spare a wounded creature that other contestants passed by (the Hare of Inaba play)
Survive a designed trial room using a gift another character gave you (the scarves of Suseri-hime)
End an encounter that could have been a fight through negotiation or settlement on camera
Return from a downed or death state and still win the same run (twice killed, twice revived)
taboos: Torment a helpless creature for the camera's amusement (his eighty brothers' crime)
Betray a party member out of jealousy
Claim an ally's kill or credit as your own
patron_block.blessing_style: Second-chance escrow: boons held in reserve that trigger at your lowest point — revives, mendings, doors that open only after apparent defeat; the god of the unseen pays out precisely when the cameras think you're done.

game_hooks: FORSAKEN LENS (census-flagged): the great dispossessed — built the land, was killed twice by his brothers and revived, then handed everything to heaven's line in the kuni-yuzuri settlement; a deposed founder whose remaining stake is the unseen world, primed to go all-in to matter again. Event hooks: en-musubi parlays (the house books side-bets pairing contestants — his matchmaking desk sets the odds); Kamiarizuki, the annual all-gods assembly at his venue, as a season-finale frame where every patron attends one table.
rating_notes: Izumo Taisha's nationwide en-musubi cult plus the Kamiarizuki all-kami assembly (verified) = influence 4; recognition 2 per census — encyclopedia-depth outside Japan, the deliberate rare-board collector entry (Zababa lesson: score the obscurity honestly, the canon makes rarity a thrill).

temperament: The agency's beloved variety regular — cast adrift by his own parents before he could walk, drifted back laughing, and now can't quite hear your bad-beat story; clap louder.

favor_conditions: Announce your bets and intentions OUT LOUD on camera before acting (he is hard of hearing — the Nishinomiya custom is to knock and clap loudly)
Laugh audibly on camera after taking a hit
Harvest a resource from a water zone at least once per run (the fisher god's tithe)
taboos: Sulk in silence after a loss — quiet prayers earn nothing from a deaf god
Mock another contestant's deformity or bad start (he was cast off for being born boneless)
patron_block.blessing_style: Windfall lag: jackpot-sized boons at random moments, always one beat late (he didn't quite hear the first time) — worthless for planning, glorious for comebacks.

game_hooks: LUCK FLAG (census) + a quiet FORSAKEN resonance: the firstborn of the creator couple, judged defective and set adrift in a reed boat — a god literally discarded by heaven who floated back and became the only luck god born on Japanese soil; the collector-beloved proof that the casino's washouts can re-enter the game. Event hook: Toka Ebisu floor event — a three-day market festival board where loud declared bets get his ear and silent grinders get nothing.
rating_notes: Nishinomiya heads ~3,500 Ebisu shrines and Toka Ebisu draws 1M+ visitors (verified) = influence 3 — large but folk-festival-shaped, honestly below Inari/Ise scale; recognition 3 (Yebisu beer, Tokyo's Ebisu district, games) — mythology-curious tier.

temperament: Headline lounge act and treasury director in one — she'll launder your winnings in holy water, double them, and curse your date on the way out.

favor_conditions: Perform mid-combat (music, dance, or flourish action) and hold a hype streak through a full fight
Wash or tithe currency at a water feature, then stake the washed sum the same day (the Zeniarai rite)
Talk an enemy or rival down instead of fighting — win an eloquence check on camera (she tamed the five-headed dragon with words)
Enter a boss arena solo
taboos: Flaunt a romance at her table (the Enoshima jealousy: she splits couples who parade before her)
Interrupt or talk over a performance
Pawn a musical instrument
patron_block.blessing_style: Flow-state artistry: multipliers that build while you keep performing and vanish the instant you play it safe — plus laundered luck: money staked after her water rite returns multiplied.

game_hooks: LUCK FLAG (census): shrines on islands where gamblers literally wash their coins to multiply them — the Zeniarai spring is a ready-made in-game mechanic (stake-laundering station that boosts the next bet). Boss-design lead: her Enoshima dragon is the template for a talk-downable boss — a monster that can be defeated by eloquence checks and audience sympathy instead of damage, converting a boss fight into a performance.
rating_notes: Thousands of Benzaiten sites with live practice (Enoshima, Itsukushima, Zeniarai coin-washing — verified) = influence 3, below the Inari/Ise-scale 4s; recognition 3 (SMT and games know Benten; below household tier). Syncretic group benzaiten_saraswati declared for Wave-4 linking to the Hindu extraction.

temperament: The pyrotechnics chief who burned down the founder's family on his first day of existence; HR keeps him on because nobody clears a stage like he does.

favor_conditions: Finish a boss with the burn condition as the killing state
Deliberately destroy cover or scenery to reshape an arena
Keep fighting while burning — never cleanse his mark before the fight ends
Forge or upgrade a blade mid-run (the sword gods sprang from his blood)
taboos: Let a fire you set spread to an ally or non-combatant (the Atago/Akiha fire-warding charter)
Quench his blessing-flame before the fight ends
Light a blaze that neither kills nor clears — fire spent on nothing
patron_block.blessing_style: Consume-to-empower: every boon burns a resource as fuel — items, terrain, your own HP — and returns triple in destructive output; the newborn god only knows how to spend.

game_hooks: FORSAKEN LENS (census-flagged): unmade at birth — his first breath killed his mother and his father beheaded him moments later, sword gods and mountain gods springing from the blood; the god whose whole existence was one catastrophic all-in, precedent for staking existence itself. Cult irony is pure casino: the arsonist retained as fire marshal (900 Atago + 800 Akiha fire-PREVENTION shrines). Enemy-design lead: burn-phase boss patterns (cf. the slice's Incinedile) where extinguishing the arena, not the monster, is the discoverable win.
rating_notes: ~900 Atago plus ~800 Akiha active fire-ward branch shrines (verified) = influence 3 — real, focused living cult below the network-4s; recognition 3 (Naruto's Kagutsuchi techniques, BlazBlue, SMT) — mythology-curious tier.

animals: white boar (his doom on Mount Ibuki)

temperament: The show's tragic GOAT — a legacy contestant whose own sponsor-father kept re-entering him in tables until one finally killed him; his highlight reel still tops the charts.
game_hooks: Contestant-legend template: epithet chains for reenactment (the Maiden-Bladed — kill a boss while disguised; Grass-Mower — escape a fire trap by clearing terrain; the White Bird — die undefeated by any contestant, only by the board). Cautionary sponsor drama: a champion run into the ground by his own backer — his arc is the house's favorite rerun about what patrons do to assets they fear. Event: a white plover crossing the arena is a canon omen graphic before legendary-difficulty spawns.
rating_notes: Continuous veneration at Atsuta (one of the five enshrined Atsuta deities — verified) and Ōtori shrines lifts him above Väinämöinen's no-cult influence 1 → 2; recognition 2, encyclopedia-depth outside Japan. Depiction low, not none: enshrined but legendary (not D-4 historical — Kojiki/Nihongi hero).

temperament: The wholesome draft-pick who built a title-winning party with snacks; merchandising loves him, the oni lobby does not.
game_hooks: Recruit-with-food mechanic seed: millet dumplings as a consumable that converts neutral beast NPCs into party allies for the rest of the run (one dumpling, one loyal companion — his exact play). Table concept: an ogre-island fortress assault board built for a small mixed party of one human and recruited beasts. Epithet template: 'Nippon-ichi' for clearing a fortress board with a full recruited-animal party and zero ally deaths.
rating_notes: Folk-tale hero, no worship = influence 1 (Baba Yaga anchor); nationally universal in Japan and internationally known folk tale = recognition 3. ip public_domain_literary per census: the standard tellings are Edo-period redactions with PD English translations (Thompson 1885, Ozaki 1903 — verified).

symbols: eight vats of eight-fold refined sake
colors: moss green (firs and cypresses grow on its back)

temperament: Eight heads, eight valleys, zero impulse control — the only table boss that will drink itself out of a championship.
game_hooks: Boss sketch — size class: colossal, multi-tile serpent spanning the arena. Eight heads as eight separately-targetable body parts, each with its own HP pool and attack pattern (maps directly onto GPT body-part targeting). Inflicts: bleeding (raking fangs) and poison (miasmal breath; per Kojiki its belly is constantly bloody and inflamed). Discoverable win condition — never a damage race: sober heads regrow and armor up; captive/NPC intel reveals the eight-vats rite — brew or loot potent sake and set eight vats at the arena's eight gates; each head that drinks gains the exhausted condition and becomes severable. Jackpot loot beat: a divine blade found only by cutting open the FOURTH tail (audience-visible discovery; loot as remnant of belief).
rating_notes: Slain monster, never worshipped = influence 1; 'Orochi' is a global games staple (KOF, Okami, Yu-Gi-Oh) = recognition 4 — the Baba Yaga pattern: famous content, zero divine wealth, normal table.

variants: The Drunken Demon of Mount Ōe

temperament: The mountain-palace whale who comps every guest to a feast — just don't ask what's in the wine, and never trust what's in yours.
game_hooks: Boss sketch — size class: large humanoid (giant in true form). Phase 1, the genteel host: the arena is a banquet and fighting him cold triggers overwhelming stats — a deliberate damage-race trap. Discoverable win: accept his hospitality (disguise/deception checks to sit at the feast — the slayers came dressed as mountain priests), then serve him the divine poison sake (quest item: jinbenkidoku-shu, granted at a shrine or by a god-tier NPC, per the otogi-zōshi), stacking poison + exhausted until his body seizes. Phase 2: the severed head stays live and BITES the head-cutter — countered by stacking looted helmets on the head slot, exactly per the legend. Inflicts bleeding (he drinks blood as wine); effectively poison-immune until the divine sake lands. Honors guest-right: violence after accepting hospitality without the sake setup triggers max wrath escalation.
rating_notes: Demon, no worship = influence 1; recognition 3 via Fate/Grand Order, Onmyoji, and yokai media — mythology-curious tier, consistent with the Väinämöinen recognition anchor.

temperament: The floor's uncontracted skill-check enforcers: they comp lessons to the humble and disappear the arrogant mid-broadcast.
game_hooks: Event/NPC hooks — (1) tengu-kakushi 'spirited away' event: the run's most arrogant contestant (highest taunt/self-hype metric) vanishes mid-floor and returns N rooms later changed — bearing either a new weapon skill or a humility curse; (2) fencing-coach NPC (the Sōjōbō pattern — the tengu king who trained Japan's most celebrated swordsman): teaches a sword skill priced in pride (permanent charm/hype reduction or a taunt ban for the run); (3) mid-boss: a crow-tengu duelist that perfect-parries over-extended attack strings — discoverable win is discipline, not damage: it disengages and yields after N clean single-strikes with no spam.
rating_notes: Yokai class, no worship (minor cult edges at Kurama honestly noted in dossier) = influence 1; tengu masks and imagery are globally recognizable through games and anime = recognition 4 — the Baba Yaga fame-without-worship pattern.

epithets_traditional: One of the Three Sacred Treasures

temperament: The house's most famous unclaimed jackpot — a wind-cutting blade that only ever turns up in the tail of something that should have stayed sober.
game_hooks: Item seed — God Relic (jackpot-rare). The Grass-Cutter: sweep attack mows all grass/soft cover in an arc and turns fire-based zone attacks back on their sender (Yamato Takeru's burning-field escape, which named the blade). Never in a loot table: obtained only by cutting open a serpent-class boss's tail (see Orochi win condition) or granted at maximum favor by an Amaterasu-tier patron. Carries a legitimacy aura: holder gains justice_oaths-tagged crowd bonuses but becomes the arena's declared target — everyone watching knows who holds the sword. OWNER NOTE: mythic blade only; the physical regalia are bound to the living imperial institution and never displayed — no imperial-ceremony framing (census flag 'restricted' recorded as data).
rating_notes: Goshintai of Atsuta Shrine — an object of continuous major living worship (verified) = influence 3 (active-cult anchor); THE legendary sword of Japanese fiction = recognition 4. Restricted recorded per census (imperial-institution linkage), collection limited to the fully public Kojiki/Nihongi mythic version.

variants: Topiltzin Ce Acatl (fused priest-king legend — the historical man stays out per D-4)

temperament: The ousted founder-CEO of the Aztec table — betrayed by the board over one bad night on camera, self-exiled east, and every quarter the tabloids ask when he's coming back. He is, and he kept the receipts.

favor_conditions: recover a fallen teammate's body or remains and carry them to the run's exit
resolve an encounter without a kill — pacify, bypass, or out-think it, on camera
solve the floor's optional puzzle or trial before engaging its boss
give a consumable to an ally while you are both below half HP
taboos: execute a surrendered or fleeing enemy on camera
consume an unidentified item offered by a dungeon NPC (his fall began with an unexamined drink)
patron_block.blessing_style: Teaches rather than smites — boons arrive as intelligence: map fragments, crafting recipes, wind-step repositioning. Payouts double on clean, killless wins.

game_hooks: Forsaken lens: a DEPOSED god — tricked with a mirror and a drink, burned his own palaces, sailed east vowing return. A comeback table hosted as his all-in is canon-perfect. Event seed: 'the unexamined drink' — a mid-run temptation event Tezcatlipoca runs against Quetzalcoatl's contracted champions; refusing it is worth affection, taking it triggers the rival-curse engine.
rating_notes: Extinct organized worship with strong cultural continuity = influence 2 (Zeus anchor); rubric §3.2 itself names Quetzalcoatl as a recognition-4 anchor.

symbols: smoking obsidian mirror (worn at the temple or in place of the torn-off foot)

temperament: The corporate raider who holds short positions on every contestant including his own — he hands you the drink, films what happens, and calls it market research.

favor_conditions: turn a rival contestant's own trap, weapon, or summon against them
win a fight in darkness or while blinded
completely change loadout or build between floors and still win the next fight
publicly humiliate a stronger contestant without dealing a single point of damage
taboos: repeat the same winning tactic on consecutive floors (he despises predictability)
thank or credit another god on camera while contracted to him
patron_block.blessing_style: A reversal-of-fortune engine — multipliers spike when your odds are worst; he will curse his own champion mid-run purely to improve the show, then pay triple if you adapt.

game_hooks: The archetypal arena-director god (canon: arenas are shaped by the judge god's psyche): obsidian-mirror arenas that replay a contestant's worst broadcast moments as hazards. He is the deal-sheet generator's stress test — pettiness 5 + fidelity 1 means courting anyone else triggers curses, and he may sell your contract mid-season for drama. Item seed: smoking-mirror scrying shards (reveal map, at the cost of showing the audience your fear).
rating_notes: Core figure of the same extinct-with-continuity corpus as Quetzalcoatl = influence 2 (Zeus anchor); known to mythology-curious audiences (Smite, myth media) but not games-tier famous = recognition 3.

temperament: The energy-drink war-sports sponsor who reads kill-feeds like quarterly earnings — on the Aztec table the sun is a subscription service and tribute is the fee.

favor_conditions: strike the first blow in a boss engagement
win a fight while outnumbered three-to-one or worse
clear an engaged fight without any party member retreating
camera-call a killing blow (dedicate the kill on air)
taboos: retreat from a fight once engaged
end a floor with zero damage dealt (his boon starves without tribute)
patron_block.blessing_style: A fed flame — combat multipliers stack with each consecutive takedown and visibly decay whenever you stop fighting; his boons are always front-line, never defensive.

game_hooks: Set-piece arena: Coatepec — a birth-ambush pyramid map where waves of star-sibling mobs (the Four Hundred Southerners) descend the steps and the win condition is holding the summit until 'dawn'. His patron economy is the purest tribute-feed loop in the roster: a boon that must be continually fed kills or it dims — great tutorial patron for the hype/spectacle meter.
rating_notes: Extinct-with-continuity core figure = influence 2 (Zeus anchor); known to mythology-curious audiences = recognition 3 (census provisional 3 confirmed).

temperament: The water-rights magnate with four reservoirs and one working tap — courteous, indispensable, and exactly one personal grievance away from raining fire on the grid.

favor_conditions: win a fight while standing in water, rain, or storm-hazard tiles
endure a chilled or suffocation condition through a full fight and still win
give water or healing consumables to a non-combatant NPC on camera
clear a flood or storm floor with no party deaths
taboos: destroy or poison a water source
hoard healing consumables while a teammate is downed (he punishes withholding with drought)
patron_block.blessing_style: Feast-or-famine, by the jar — one boon in four is torrential abundance, the other three are tests (fire-rain, blight-rain, flint-rain); everything he gives lands area-wide, never single-target.

game_hooks: Floor modifier: 'the Four Jars' — a rain-roulette where each storm floor draws one of four rains (blessed growth / burn hazard / infected blight / bleeding flint-shard rain), mapping directly onto rulebook conditions. His paradise Tlalocan seeds an afterlife-lobby event: contestants downed by water or lightning hazards get a Tlalocan interlude with a gift, everyone else gets nothing — the audience loves the unfairness.
rating_notes: Extinct-with-continuity core figure (Templo Mayor co-tenant) = influence 2; mythology-curious recognition = 3, per census and Zeus/Väinämöinen anchors.

temperament: The collections department made flesh — every soul is a receivable, every trial has fine print, and he smiles because the paperwork always favors the house.

favor_conditions: finish a run after having been downed at least once
beat a rigged or 'impossible' trial by out-cheating the house
collect every corpse-drop on a floor before advancing
taboos: use a resurrection item (souls leave his ledger only by his say)
break a bargain struck with any dungeon NPC
patron_block.blessing_style: Names the price before the boon, always; his strongest multipliers only arm while you are one hit from death — he pays best at the door of his own house.

game_hooks: The house's rigged-trial designer: the 'conch with no holes' is a whole puzzle archetype — trials that are unwinnable as stated and must be beaten by out-cheating the house (worms and bees as the solution template). As a table boss he never blocks you outright; he agrees, attaches an impossible condition, and sends quail when you succeed anyway. Strong candidate for the canon's fallen-god game-runner bench (Enki pattern).
rating_notes: Extinct-with-continuity core figure = influence 2; Day-of-the-Dead-adjacent visibility keeps him at mythology-curious recognition 3 (census provisional 3).

variants: Xochipilli (heavily overlapping aspect — kept separate per census; Wave 4 to rule)

temperament: The lounge god of the casino floor — five flowers in his hatband, zero savings, all-in on the next hand; even his curses are just content.

favor_conditions: stake loot at an in-dungeon game of chance and let the result stand
announce a low-odds play on camera before attempting it, and land it
celebrate a win with a showboat emote on camera
taboos: renege on a declared wager
abandon a self-imposed challenge condition mid-run (his feast demands the fast be kept)
patron_block.blessing_style: A pure variance amplifier — widens both tails of every roll you make; his punishments are never lethal, only humiliating (boils, piles, and the camera zooming in).

game_hooks: CASINO NOBILITY — the census's flagged luck_gambling god, attested presiding over a patolli game in the Codex Magliabechiano with winnings treated as his gift; Durán and Sahagún record players staking homes, land, and their own freedom. Natural in-dungeon patolli dealer NPC. Forsaken lens: an influence-1 pauper with risk_appetite 5 — the first god in line to stake his own existence on a table, exactly the §5-amendment profile.
rating_notes: Zababa-anchor honesty: extinct minor cult, encyclopedia-depth fame = influence 1, recognition 2 (a notch above Zababa purely on games-history visibility).

temperament: The getaway driver of the pantheon — engine idling outside every doomed table; not a coward, just the only god who reads the odds and loves you anyway.

favor_conditions: escape a fight you cannot win and still clear the floor
escort a downed or crippled teammate to safety
change form, stance, or loadout mid-fight to survive it
taboos: mock another contestant's retreat or cowardice on camera
leave a teammate's body behind on an extracted floor
patron_block.blessing_style: Escape-hatch boons — movement surges, feign-death, carry-speed while hauling an ally; strongest when you run to save something, inert when you grandstand.

game_hooks: Forsaken lens (census flag): a god who was UNMADE — he wept, fled his own sacrifice through three transformations, and was caught and killed anyway. A Forsaken table hosted by Xolotl would be built entirely on escape, and his hosting reads as desperation, not appetite (risk 1 god going all-in = the amendment's tragic case). Event seed: 'the god who begs you to hide him' — protect a disguised NPC through a floor without revealing him to the cameras.
rating_notes: Minor extinct cult = influence 1 (Zababa-side honesty; continuity survives mainly through the axolotl/xolo dog names); axolotl-era pop visibility puts him at mythology-curious recognition 3 (census provisional 3).

epithets_traditional: Tonatiuh, the Fifth Sun (what he became)

symbols: offerings of reeds, maguey spines, and his own scabs
animals: eagle (followed him into the fire and charred its wings)

temperament: The janitor who jumped into the furnace when the headliner choked four times — now the whole building runs on his light, and he still eats in the staff canteen.

favor_conditions: commit your rarest consumable or item to a single decisive attempt
volunteer first for the floor's designated trial or hazard
win a fight while carrying the burn or infected condition
taboos: decline a challenge you already accepted on camera (he watched the rich god flinch four times)
mock a weaker contestant's gear, body, or looks
patron_block.blessing_style: One boon, once, everything — a single colossal multiplier that arms only on an irreversible commitment; no partial payouts, no second charge.

game_hooks: THE Forsaken exemplar of the whole census: a god with nothing who went all-in with his own body and won the biggest pot in the cosmos (he IS the current sun). His story is also the canon's mortal-to-god pipeline in divine miniature — enter as the lowest, leave owning the lights. Table seed: a two-brazier trial floor where a rich rival visibly flinches four times and the first contestant to jump takes everything. Wave-4 question: whether Tonatiuh should ever be a separate entity (recommend: no — same being, post-jackpot).
rating_notes: Zababa-anchor floor: extinct, no independent cult, encyclopedia-depth = influence 1, recognition 2; his value is narrative fit, not fame — the canon's rare-board collector case.

epithets_traditional: the triad: Thunderbolt Huracan, Newborn Thunderbolt, Sudden Thunderbolt

symbols: the word spoken over the water

temperament: The three-voiced founder who has already recalled two entire product lines of humanity for shipping without gratitude installed; his patch notes are floods.

favor_conditions: strip a boss's buffs, adornments, or trophy objects before killing it (pride goes first)
destroy a spawner or corrupted construct completely, so nothing respawns behind you
credit your teammates by name on camera after a victory (he works as a triad, never alone)
taboos: claim on camera that you needed no one (Seven Macaw's sin)
leave a corrupted construct 'mostly dead' and walk on
patron_block.blessing_style: Speaks the arena into new shapes — boons land as weather and terrain edits (fog walls, lightning-marked tiles, flash floods) rather than personal buffs.

game_hooks: Arena weather-director god: his psyche-shaped arenas are unfinished drafts of worlds — mud people dissolving in the rain, wooden mannequin mobs whose own tools and animals turn on them (the wooden people's fate as an enemy faction concept). The wipe-and-retry creation loop is a diegetic frame for run resets. Depiction note: recorded living (named in contemporary K'iche' daykeeper prayer); policy v2 = no gate, portray respectfully.
rating_notes: Continuous minority folk practice (daykeepers still invoke Popol Vuh creator forces) = influence 3, below Amaterasu's organized-4 and above extinct-2; hurricane etymology plus Popol Vuh fame = recognition 3.

temperament: The whales every pit boss prays never walk in: they lose the first hand on purpose, and by the last hand the house is signing its own liquidation.
game_hooks: The archetypal legendary contestants — the pair who beat the house at its own rigged game, died on purpose, and came back disguised to make the owners beg for their own elimination. Epithet-template gold: 'House-Breaker' chains (scout the lords' names → refuse rigged hospitality → deliberate loss → disguised return). As NPC legends they appear as an unassuming duo of street performers whose show always ends with somebody's empire gone.
rating_notes: Väinämöinen anchor exactly: epic-hero fame inside a living culture but no cult = influence 1; Popol Vuh is widely taught = recognition 3.

temperament: Disgraced former owners of the deepest table in the building — banned from hosting after they bet the house on a magic act and asked, on the record, to be part of the trick.
game_hooks: Boss-pair sketch: Medium humanoid duo running a trial-house gauntlet; inflicted conditions map to Xibalba's houses — chilled (Cold House), bleeding (Razor and Bat Houses), burn (the torch trick), infected (the putrescence lords). NEVER a damage race — discoverable win: (1) scout their true names pre-fight (send a summon to sting each lord so the others cry his name; named lords lose invulnerability and mannequin decoys are exposed); (2) refuse three rigged hospitality offers; (3) final phase, stage the resurrection show — the lords demand it be done to them, and only their own volunteered sacrifice can kill them. Forsaken lens: the house that went all-in and was unmade — the myth even ends with their worship revoked, a god-bankruptcy on the record.
rating_notes: Zababa-side: specialists-plus fame only = influence 1, recognition 2; their value is the luck_gambling + forsaken census flags, not celebrity.

symbols: a severed head as a ballgame ball

temperament: Night-shift security in the lightless wing — the cameras only ever get one frame of him, and someone's headless silhouette.
game_hooks: Enemy sketch: Medium-Large flyer, elite/miniboss of darkness floors. Condition affinity: bleeding (decapitating snatch strikes). Fight structure from the myth: total-darkness phases in which anything that moves or 'peeks' is struck (the Twins survived the night hiding inside their blowguns — forced-stillness rounds); discoverable win condition: bait him with a decoy head (the carved-squash substitution) — he only becomes targetable while gloating over the false head. Punishes impatience, never a damage race.
rating_notes: Influence 1 (monster, no cult); recognition 3 — census provisional 3, sustained by videogame presence (Smite) and steady pop-myth circulation.

variants: Tlaltecuhtli (the conflated earth-lord form in Histoyre du Mechique)

temperament: Not a guest — the foundation. The first table was built on her back, and she is still owed a foot.
game_hooks: Enemy sketch: COLOSSAL terrain boss — the arena IS her back, and every doorway is a joint-mouth. Condition affinities: crushed (bite, rolling mass), suffocation (dragged under the water table). Discoverable win condition straight from the myth: she only surfaces for offered flesh — a contestant must deliberately stake a body part (limb-HP system synergy: the Tezcatlipoca gambit) or a treasured equipped item as bait; her heart-node is exposed only during the bite. Finish requires a two-point 'tear' objective executed from opposite map edges (the two serpent-form gods). Damage outside these windows heals into new terrain.
rating_notes: Influence 1 (primordial monster, no cult); recognition 2 — encyclopedia-depth, known mainly to mythology readers (Zababa-adjacent honesty).

epithets_traditional: The Honored Ones of the Crossroads

temperament: The night-shift floor staff who died on the job and were promoted to escort the sun — kind to the desperate, merciless at crossroads, five nights a year.
game_hooks: Event/NPC seeds: calendar-timed crossroads apparitions — on the five marked day-sign floors (1 Deer, 1 Rain, 1 Monkey, 1 House, 1 Eagle as floor modifiers) they manifest at crossroads tiles at dusk. Leave an offering: gain their escort (guaranteed safe extraction — they escort the sun, they can escort you). Refuse: a madness_dream curse expressed through Forced Action d6 penalties and the theft of your smallest equipped item (they died reaching for children and came away with empty arms). Bonus texture: they judge women contestants who die mid-run as fallen warriors, with honors.
rating_notes: Influence 1 (divinized ghosts, past cult of appeasement extinct); recognition 2 — encyclopedia-depth (census provisional 2), with La Llorona adjacency noted but not claimed.

temperament: The house's trophy-case weapon — the sun's own dart, signed out exactly once per apocalypse, and it remembers being thrown.
game_hooks: Item seed — God Relic tier (jackpot-rare per canon loot rules; must feel like a remnant of belief, and this one is the sun's own weapon): a living turquoise serpent-lance. Applies burn on hit; charges from spectacle (hype/camera meter); at full charge, one guaranteed dismembering sweep (auto-severs a limb on a non-boss — Coyolxauhqui callback). Drawback: starved of kills or spotlight it coils inert and inflicts burn on its own wielder each turn it is carried cold.
rating_notes: Influence 1 (artifact, no cult of its own); recognition 2 — encyclopedia-depth; personality axes here model the relic's behavior as a semi-sentient weapon (loyal, wrathful, demanding), per the artifact-record convention.

symbols: lapis measuring rod and line (Descent gear)

temperament: The founding family's ambitious heiress — walks into any boardroom wearing whatever she took from the last one, and has already sat in your chair.

favor_conditions: Win a fight the odds board flagged as beyond your rating
Take a boss's or rival contestant's signature equipment and wear or use it on camera in the same run
Return to a room where you were previously downed and clear it
Hold the run's highest single-encounter hype/camera-call gain

Credit another god on camera for a kill her boon powered

As forsaken_host her fine print says someone else can be made to cover her losses. His whole myth-sheet supports it: he beats every crisis with a loophole, never a hammer — swore not to tell a human about the flood, so he told the reed wall the human slept behind. Dealer/forsaken_host roles reflect canon, not just myth. Event seeds: the leaked-apocalypse-memo (an NPC knows the wipe mechanic early); the turtle-pit (a trap that humbles whoever hoards an objective item). His Adapa myth also seeds a cruel event: refuse the offered 'bread of life' consumable on bad advice and lose the run's best buff.
rating_notes: Influence 2 = revival target per tradition census (Temple of Sumer); recognition 3 is the spec rubric's own named anchor for Enki (§3.2).

The Erra epic is his built-in downfall arc: flattery pulls him off his throne and the table descends into chaos — a live event where the reigning table sponsor is lured away mid-season.

Cast him as a Normal-table dealer whose one all-in episode is a rare board event; his eagle-headed staff is the obvious relic drop ('remnants of belief' loot rule). NOT patron_capable — no narrative myths survive to source a deal sheet from, and inventing one would break the no-invention bar.

His Atrahasis episode seeds a discoverable event solution: when the plague officer comes, tribute HIM alone and he withdraws his hand, ashamed. His snubbing (Nergal's failure to stand) shows protocol-pettiness 4: disrespect him and the underworld invoices you.

DISCOVERABLE WIN CONDITION straight from Enuma Elish: her exterior is effectively invulnerable — she becomes targetable only when she opens her mouth to devour; bait the devour, wedge the maw open with a wind/gale effect, strike the inside (Marduk's storm-wind + arrow kill). FORSAKEN LENS: the corpus's primal all-in loss — an unmade god whose erased body became the playing field, the exact 'erasure unlocks new stages' precedent canon wants. Epithet seed: 'He Who Saw the Deep' for completing the full chain. His returning-home-empty-handed-and-wise ending is the canonical 'losing run that still becomes a legend' — exactly the myth-recreation economy's point.

His death is the corpus's 'gods' invoice' — the gods decree ONE of the pair must die for the Bull and Humbaba, and it isn't the king — seeding a brutal co-op event: a shared kill generates a debt exactly one party member can pay. Fidelity 5 (dies for his friend's deeds), pettiness 3 (hurled the Bull's haunch in a goddess's face on camera — the single most casino-native gesture in the corpus).

Post-fight moral fork: he begs for his life on camera — sparing vs executing splits god favor (executing him angered Enlil in the myth), a bosses-with-consequences template.

temperament: The valet who watched the owner bathe, grabbed the master deed off the nightstand, and went all-in on being uncatchable — for exactly one afternoon of owning the universe.

FORSAKEN LENS (census flag): the all-in heist archetype — bet everything on one theft, lost its wings; a natural heist-event boss whose loot is whatever objective the table stole from the players.

Wearing it, fate decrees FOR you: declared-action requirements relax (the sim's auto-succeed grammar bent one notch further), enemy 'reversion' auras (Anzu-style) are ignored. The cost is its myth-attested fidelity-1 nature: it serves whoever wears it and betrays every holder in sequence (Enlil → Anzu → Qingu → Marduk) — equipping it broadcasts your position table-wide, spawns theft events, and on your death it instantly empowers the taker. 'Whoever wears the deed IS the house — and everyone knows it.' Satisfies the loot hard rule: it is literally a remnant of belief in bureaucratized fate.
rating_notes: Influence 1 (object, not worshipped); recognition 2 = census 2 — famous inside the corpus, encyclopedia-depth outside it.
