game_hooks: The canon's rare-board collector piece: a mythic-obscurity goddess whose single great myth is beating a ruler's bounded grant — 'as much land as four oxen plough in a day and a night' — by yoking four shapeshifted giant sons and ploughing Zealand out of Sweden's body. Honesty note: primary sources frame it as a gamed GRANT, not a coin-flip wager (census one-liner corrected). Bonus depth: in Lokasenna 21 Odin declares she knows all fate as well as he does — a sleeper wisdom claim that makes her a quietly terrifying odds-reader. Event seed: 'The Plough Clause' — a reward that scales with literal-wording exploitation.
rating_notes: Zealand-bound local memory ≈ Zababa's honest floor = influence 1; encyclopedia-depth = recognition 2 — a collector's thrill when she bids, per canon. Census 'wager' framing corrected against Gylfaginning 1 / Ynglinga saga 5.

temperament: The rookie phenom every investor wants a piece of — killed the vault-guard dragon from a hole in the floor, ate the insider knowledge raw, and never noticed the curse clause riding the payout.
game_hooks: Epithet template 'Fáfnir's-Bane': ambush a beast-class boss from concealment below (the trench), then consume part of it to unlock hidden intelligence — the heart-blood taste granting bird-speech maps cleanly to 'revealed enemy intents/telegraphs for the rest of the run.' The hoard he wins carries a cursed affix that transfers on pickup (Andvaranaut seed): the audience-favorite destroyed by his own winnings is premium cautionary spectacle, and the Legendary NPC contestant version of him should always be mid-arc — beloved, rich, and already doomed.
rating_notes: No worship = influence 1; recognition 4 sits one above the Väinämöinen-3 hero anchor, carried by Wagner's Ring, the Fate franchise, and decades of games — inside the ±1 band. Census ip flag honored: the legend survives via literary redactions (Völsunga saga, Nibelungenlied), all PD.

symbols: Grendel's arm nailed beneath the gable

temperament: Old-school showman who fights the main event bare-handed because the ratings demand fairness — then takes the final table at retirement age knowing the buy-in is his life.
game_hooks: Epithet template 'Grendel's-Bane': defeat a fiend-class enemy with no weapon equipped, matching the enemy's own handicap — the poem frames the weaponless boast as deliberate sportsmanship, i.e. a pure audience-hype play the casino would adore. Second template, 'The Last Board': an aged legend enters one final run knowing the odds, wins the objective, and dies of the win (the dragon fight) — the template for tragic-spectacle finales and a mortal-side mirror of a Forsaken all-in.
rating_notes: No worship = influence 1; recognition 4 (global curriculum staple plus film adaptations) — one above hero-anchor Väinämöinen, inside the ±1 band. PD literary epic; ship freely.

temperament: The collateral that outgrew the vault — every fetter the house breaks on him compounds the debt, and he knows exactly whose hand is still owed.
game_hooks: Enemy sketch — size class: huge, growing to colossal by phase (the myth's engine: he grows, the gods panic). Condition affinities: bleeding (rending bite), crushed (jaw pressure), exhausted (relentless-pursuit aura stacking on fleeing targets). Discoverable win condition: he cannot be damage-raced — while unbound his wounds knit; the arena scatters six 'impossible' components (a cat's footfall, a woman's beard, a mountain's roots, bear's sinews, fish's breath, bird's spittle) that craft the fetter, and the bind only holds if a volunteer places an arm in his jaws as pledge — that arm takes the run-permanent disabled condition when the trick is revealed (the Tyr toll). Each failed fetter attempt raises his size class and damage. The audience pays for the sacrifice, not the kill.
rating_notes: Beast, no cult = influence 1; the recognition-4 rubric anchor literally names 'Loki's children.' Boss design honors the bosses-are-never-damage-races hard rule by construction.

temperament: The house perimeter made flesh — the only player whose position IS the table, and when he finally cashes out, the room floods.
game_hooks: Enemy sketch — size class: colossal, arena-scale; only coil segments ever enter play, because his body IS the arena boundary (the ouroboros ring). Condition affinities: poison (venom-spray pools, the Ragnarök killer), suffocation (coil constriction), crushed (segment slams). Discoverable win condition: there is no total HP pool at all — the objective sits beyond the ring, and the discovery is that the boundary is alive; bait a strike with a decoy (the ox-head echo of Thor's fishing trip) so the tail releases and the ring opens for extraction. Optional glory-kill trap: destroying a segment triggers a venom flood over the kill zone — Thor's nine dying steps, weaponized against damage-race players.
rating_notes: Beast, no cult = influence 1; same 'Loki's children' recognition-4 anchor as Fenrir. Segments-not-boss design keeps the no-damage-race rule structural.

symbols: drinking horn plumbed to the ocean
giant's glove mistaken for a hall

temperament: Ran the only pit in nine worlds that took Thor's money and lived — every game rigged, every prop a metaphor, and the house's one rule is that you never find the same casino twice.
game_hooks: Boss sketch — the canon's arena-from-the-judge's-psyche rule made literal: a large-size illusionist giant-king running a rigged game-show gauntlet. Condition affinities: exhausted (every rigged contest drains; failure adds exhausted stacks), chilled (frost-giant hall). Discoverable win condition: every posted contest is unwinnable as presented because each opponent is a disguised universal — the drinking horn is the sea, the racing boy is Thought, the eating rival is Wildfire, the cat is the World Serpent, the crone is Old Age; victory is IDENTIFICATION, not performance: inspect props, gather clues, and NAME the true referent on camera before wagering — a named illusion collapses and pays out; three collapses end the show and the castle vanishes (he is never actually present to kill). Forsaken-host justification: census-flagged — his format IS the Forsaken pattern, solo trials designed to be lost.
rating_notes: Encyclopedia-depth = recognition 2, influence 1 (Zababa floor logic) — but casino-frame value is outsized: myth's most explicit rigged-game host, selected over higher-recognition Surtr/Ymir for exactly that reason.

symbols: water and clay poured on the world-tree
animals: swan (the pair bred in Urðr's well)

temperament: The actuarial department upstairs — they set every contestant's opening odds before the gods even see the board, accept no tips, comp no one, and their line has never once moved.
game_hooks: Run-start event desk: 'the Thread Draw' — each contestant draws a fate-thread (a run modifier: good, bad, or strange) that NO god's boon can reroll or buy off — the one subsystem patron multipliers cannot touch, giving the luck economy an honest floor and making patron gods visibly subordinate to the house's actuaries. Accepting a bad thread without complaint on camera earns audience-sympathy hype. luck_gambling flag per §4: fate-office casino nobility, above even the gods' appeals — flagged as required.
rating_notes: Mythology-curious = recognition 3 (Väinämöinen tier); no cult = influence 1. Census luck_gambling flag honored — every such figure extracted per §4's standing order.

temperament: Not a player — the chip: Norse luck as a heritable, lendable balance that walks a half-step behind its owner, and the sharpest lobbies in the casino bid on estates just to hire one away.
game_hooks: Mechanic/item seed rather than an NPC: a per-contestant Luck meter with Norse skin — accumulates on honorable wins, spendable to nudge a Forced Action d6, and crucially TRANSFERABLE: a dying contestant can will their hamingja to a teammate on camera (one-time full transfer; guaranteed spectacle beat), mirroring the attested saga practice of inheriting and even LENDING luck (Hjalti Skeggiason borrowed King Olaf's hamingja for a voyage). Loot seed: 'Hamingja Heirloom' follower-relic carrying a dead legend's accumulated luck — belief-remnant loot by definition. Event seed: the Estate Auction — the house auctions a bankrupt god's or dead contestant's hamingja to the floor. No casino_role fits an ambient asset; deliberately left empty.
rating_notes: Specialists only = 1/1 — Zababa's honest floor — collected deliberately because canon prizes mythic-tier rarities and this one is casino-native: luck as a transferable, spendable, inheritable asset. Census luck_gambling flag honored.

symbols: the six impossible ingredients (cat's footfall, woman's beard, mountain's roots, bear's sinews, fish's breath, bird's spittle)

temperament: The house's finest instrument of debt collection — looks like a hair ribbon, holds like the end of the world, and cost exactly one honest god's hand to deploy.
game_hooks: Item seed — god-relic tier bind item: assembled from a six-component arena fetch where every component is an 'impossible' obtained by lateral thinking, not combat (record a cat's silent footfall, coax a beard from a mountain-crone NPC, bottle a fish's breath...). On use, permanently BINDS one beast-class boss instead of killing it: the boss becomes a bound arena fixture and its loot table converts to a slow tribute trickle. Activation cost: a volunteer must place an arm in the target's jaws — that arm takes the run-permanent disabled condition; the audience hype spike prices the sacrifice, not the kill. Belief-remnant loot rule satisfied: it is the gods' own broken promise, wearable.
rating_notes: Artifact, encyclopedia-depth = recognition 2, influence floor 1. Personality axes are nominal (schema requires them for all classes) — read them as the item's behavior profile: absolute strictness/fidelity, zero appetite.

epithets_traditional: Māui of the topknot of Taranga

animals: kererū (wood pigeon, his transformation in Grey)

temperament: The clip-show legend every rookie studies — cheated the sun's shot clock, fished new tables up from nothing, and died mid-heist trying to rob the house of death itself.
game_hooks: THE contestant_legend template: the trickster archetype whose myth-recreations anchor the epithet economy (Sun-Snarer: win a timed objective by disabling its timer mechanism; Land-Fisher: raise terrain to create a route; Fire-Thief: drain a boss's resource pool to its last charge and survive the tantrum). Forsaken lens: his death is the canon cautionary tape — a mortal-side all-in against the house of death, lost on a companion's stifled laugh; the casino replays it before every VVIP game. Appears as legendary NPC/ghost commentator. ART NOTE: Disney's Moana design is protected IP — build from traditional carving/kōwhaiwhai depiction instead.
rating_notes: Väinämöinen anchor: hero of a living culture but no worship = influence 1; Moana made him a global household name = recognition 5 (Zeus tier); the figure is traditional, only Disney's specific design is off-limits.

epithets_traditional: Ka wahine ʻai honua (the earth-eating woman)

temperament: The casino's pyrotechnics director on a permanent short fuse — comps you a suite for sharing your dinner, torches the wing if you pocket a souvenir.

favor_conditions: Make an offering (discard a valuable item) at a fire or lava hazard before engaging that zone's boss
Give food or supplies to a stranger NPC who asks for them on camera
Finish a run holding more captured territory/zones than any rival team
Win a formal one-on-one challenge that you yourself issued
taboos: Loot anything from a shrine or volcanic terrain without leaving an offering in its place
Refuse a direct request for food or aid from a disguised/low-status NPC
Extinguish a fire objective she has marked as hers
patron_block.blessing_style: Terrain-scale patronage: her boons reshape the arena itself — lava flows that cut enemy paths, fresh ground raised under her champion's feet — arriving suddenly and excessively, like an eruption.

game_hooks: Natural arena-director god (canon: arenas mirror the director's psyche): a volcano table whose terrain she edits live according to her mood meter. Her deal sheet is the hospitality-test loop — disguised-NPC encounters that pay out huge for generosity and mark you for wrath on refusal. 'Pele's curse' (souvenir rocks mailed back to Hawaiʻi) is a ready-made loot-taboo mechanic.
rating_notes: Below Amaterasu (4, organized national practice): continuous but diffuse folk veneration (documented 2018 Kīlauea hoʻokupu, Merrie Monarch offerings) = influence 3, the tradition ceiling; volcano-goddess pop presence just under household tier = recognition 4 (Baba Yaga anchor).

temperament: Old-money owner of the aquarium floor, still litigating a custody feud with his forest brother over who keeps the fish.

favor_conditions: Discard/return the first loot drop of the run as a first-catch offering
Win an encounter fought in or over water terrain
Kill a land beast that has fled from a water zone (deliver the runaways back to him)
taboos: Ally mid-run with a contestant under Tāne's contract (the ancient grievance)
Strip a water zone's resources beyond the objective quota (break the rāhui)
patron_block.blessing_style: Tidal: boons arrive in wave-sets — several small multipliers in quick succession, then a withdrawn lull; sends sea-beast kin as temporary allies; everything recedes at once if a taboo stirs him.

game_hooks: Flood events: mid-run water-level changes that reshape the board (his revenge on the land). Faction spill-over engine with Tāne — the two contracts are mechanically hostile, and courting one auto-provokes rival-curse rolls from the other (both at pettiness 4). Syncretic skins: Kanaloa (deep-ocean/squid), Taʻaroa (world-shell creator), Tagaloa (sky-sender) as archipelago variants of one playable identity.
rating_notes: Influence 3: living karakia to Tangaroa before sea ventures and in Māori public ritual = continuous folk practice (above Zeus's 2, below Amaterasu's 4); mythology-curious recognition = 3 (Väinämöinen anchor).

epithets_traditional: Tāne te wānanga (Tāne of sacred knowledge)

symbols: the climbing way to the twelfth heaven

temperament: The green-tech magnate who pulled the knowledge heist of the age — three briefcases from the top floor — and expects his champions to read the file before the fight.

favor_conditions: Learn a boss's weakness or win condition before engaging it
Reach an objective via a climb/ascent route instead of the ground route
Complete a preparation step the game marks as optional before a major fight
Free a caged or trapped creature on camera
taboos: Destroy flora terrain outside objectives (needless burning/felling — the Rātā lesson)
Skip an offered preparation/scouting prompt and engage a boss blind
patron_block.blessing_style: Front-loads knowledge, not stats: reveals maps, weaknesses, and hidden paths — three baskets' worth of intel, each earned separately; his birds serve as scouts.

game_hooks: The intel-economy patron — the Odin-analog seat on this table's roster. Event: 'The Ascent' — an optional high-route dungeon branch with Whiro-sent harassment waves (reenacting the climb) that pays out a basket (full boss dossier) at the top. His feud with Tangaroa and cold war with Whiro drive faction spill-over.
rating_notes: Influence 3: living forest karakia and mātauranga practice = continuous folk practice (Zeus 2 < Tāne 3 < Amaterasu 4); baskets-of-knowledge story is NZ-curriculum famous but global reach is mythology-curious = recognition 3.

variants: Hine-tītama (her former name, the Dawn Maiden)
epithets_traditional: guardian of the dead at the door of Rarohenga

temperament: The final cashier at the last window; she doesn't gamble, she settles — every chip in the building crosses her counter eventually.

favor_conditions: Give a fallen contestant's body a marked rite/pause on camera before moving on
Decline a revival or second-chance offer when one is presented
Kill an enemy in a single strike while it sleeps or is unaware
taboos: Use resurrection items or death-cheating mechanics (Māui's sin)
Loot or mock the dead disrespectfully on camera
Wake a sleeping boss through carelessness (noise/laugh triggers — the fantail rule)
patron_block.blessing_style: The house edge: no flashy gifts — her champion simply never suffers the worst outcome; killing blows against them mysteriously downgrade, and her multipliers pay only on clean, final kills.

game_hooks: Forsaken lens, inverted: she is the table where a legend went all-in against death and THE HOUSE WON — the only mythologically proven undefeated host, making her the natural adjudicator/host of VVIP death-games (risk_appetite 1: she only ever bets on the sure thing, and she IS the sure thing). Her patronage is the anti-gambler's deal sheet — a fascinating contrast pick on a casino roster.
rating_notes: Influence 2: strong living cultural continuity (tangihanga references) but she is feared/acknowledged rather than petitioned — below the invoked atua at 3, above Zababa's floor; recognition 3 via the famous death-of-Māui story (Väinämöinen tier).

symbols: snares and nets (his rites govern hunting and fishing)

temperament: Head of casino security and owner of the MMA franchise — the New Zealand Army literally calls itself his tribe; he respects anyone who won't back up a step.

favor_conditions: Win a fight while outnumbered without retreating a single tile
Defeat an enemy using a snare or trap you set yourself
Personally land the killing blow on every boss of the run
Harvest/consume a beaten elite enemy for resources on camera (making the tapu common)
taboos: Flee a fight your team could still win while any ally stands
Accept a truce or parley with an enemy faction mid-run (peace belongs to his brother Rongo, not to him)
patron_block.blessing_style: Combat multipliers that scale with disadvantage — the fewer allies standing and the worse the odds, the harder his champion hits; total silence when you are winning comfortably.

game_hooks: The last-man-standing patron: his myth (alone and unbowed against the storm god, then eating his cowardly brothers' children) is a ready-made comeback-mechanic license — bonuses that only exist below 50% party strength. Modern-life attribution lands itself: the actual NZ Army is 'Ngāti Tūmatauenga', so at the casino he runs security and the fight card.
rating_notes: Influence 3: uniquely concrete living invocation — a state army founded as his iwi in 1994 plus living karakia = continuous practice; recognition 2 (encyclopedia-depth outside NZ), so Normal table per §3.5 despite the résumé.

symbols: akua loa standard (crossbar pole with white kapa banner)

temperament: VP of Events & Festival Season: suspends all wars, opens all books, rains comps on the floor, and referees the boxing card he made you bet on.

favor_conditions: Win a formal minigame or side-contest inside the run (race, target contest, arena sport) with a stake wagered on yourself
Declare a truce window on camera and hold it — complete an objective segment with zero kills
Enter a zone's optional bonus wager and clear it (grow the pot)
Heal or feed a wounded rival contestant
taboos: Initiate combat during your own declared truce window (breaking the Makahiki kapu)
Strike a surrendered or helpless opponent in anger (the Kaikilani sin — the one he cannot forgive because it is his own)
patron_block.blessing_style: Runs the season, not the fight: converts spectacle into stakes — his champion's side-bets pay multiplied, supply drops turn abundant, and healing falls like rain between fights.

game_hooks: THE luck_gambling flag-holder of this tradition (casino nobility per §4): Makahiki was a real annual season when war stopped and the islands turned to boxing, wrestling, sledding and surfing with heavy wagering — stakes up to and including lives. Event design: 'Festival Season' table state — combat locked, forced-minigame rounds with mandatory betting, Lono refereeing as dealer. His grief-origin (instituted the games in his dead wife's honor, then departed promising return — the myth Cook sailed into) gives the character a mournful edge under the party-planner surface.
rating_notes: Influence 3: organized annual Makahiki ceremonies revived since 1982 (Kahoʻolawe, Protect Kahoʻolawe ʻOhana) = organized revival tier, consistent with Ásatrú-scale anchor; recognition 2 — Cook-contact fame is history-buff, not pop-culture.

epithets_traditional: keeper of the fingernails of fire

symbols: kaikōmako wood (where her last fire lives)

temperament: Burned-out high roller conned out of nine stakes by one grinning grandchild; deals at the ember tables now with her last nail in her pocket, itching to go all-in.

favor_conditions: Spend a limited-use resource to save another contestant
Commit your last consumable or charge to a single attack that wins the fight (go all-in)
Carry an unextinguished flame objective through a burn-hazard zone to its end
taboos: Ask her for a boon twice in one run for the same problem (she was conned by repeat requests once; never again)
Extinguish a fire you could have carried or used (waste of her gift)
patron_block.blessing_style: Everything at once: enormous burn-tagged multipliers that consume themselves — each boon is a fingernail, she only has so many left, and her contract visibly depletes as she gives.

game_hooks: Prime Forsaken-host candidate per the §5 amendment: a goddess who staked her whole treasury on one grinning guest and lost nine-tenths of it — desperate, nearly bankrupt, nothing-to-lose, and mythologically ESTABLISHED as willing to throw her last fire after a loss (she nearly burned the world with it). Deal sheet writes itself: high-variance, self-depleting boons. As fallen-god staff (canon §4: bankrupt gods run the games), she deals at burn-table events.
rating_notes: Influence 2: no organized veneration but strong living story continuity (NZ curriculum staple) = extinct-worship-strong-continuity tier (Zeus anchor); recognition 2, encyclopedia/NZ-schoolroom depth.

animals: lizards and tuatara (his earthly manifestations, tapu-feared)

temperament: The basement floor boss who lost the knowledge bid, took the service elevator down for good, and mails plagues upstairs while he waits out the house.
game_hooks: BOSS SKETCH — large-class darkness lord. Affinities: inflicts infected and poison (his Maiki disease-brood are the personified sicknesses), plus dissolution (psychic dread of the dark); lizard minion swarms inflict bleeding. Discoverable win condition (never a damage race): the arena holds three sealed light/knowledge caches — a basket-heist reenactment; damage only sticks to Whiro while a cache is open and shining, and each opened basket strips a darkness phase. Forsaken lens: he wagered everything on beating Tāne to the summit of the heavens, lost, and was banished below — a deposed god gnawing at the world's foundations to fund a comeback; per canon §4 (bankrupt gods run games), he hosts the underworld tables.
rating_notes: Influence 1: feared and averted, never worshipped — no divine bankroll (Zababa floor logic); recognition 2 via NZ cultural presence (Whiro also names the dark night of the lunar calendar).

temperament: The contestant who actually read the terms and conditions — followed the ritual line by line up the vine while flashier climbers fell past him.
game_hooks: Contestant-legend template for the rules-lawyer build: his ascent succeeded because he followed the ritual instructions EXACTLY where his brother Karihi grabbed the wrong vine and fell — epithet template 'Rule-Reader' (clear a gauntlet by honoring every optional ritual prompt where a rival skipped them). His Ponaturi revenge (sealed house, dawn light) doubles as the tutorial legend for the Ponaturi enemy race's win condition. Avenging-son arc + lightning finisher = high-spectacle NPC legend.
rating_notes: Väinämöinen anchor exactly: revered hero-ancestor, no cult = influence 1; known at encyclopedia depth = recognition 2.

epithets_traditional: the pale sea-goblins who sleep ashore

temperament: Night-shift mooks of the drowned floor — brave as a pack till someone reaches for the house lights.
game_hooks: ENEMY SKETCH — medium humanoid swarm, spawns from water tiles at night. Affinities: inflict bleeding (mob claws) and chilled (cold sea grip); vulnerable to burn. Discoverable win condition: sunlight/strong light kills them outright — reenact Tāwhaki's trick by sealing their retreat and opening a light source (doors propped at dawn, shutters thrown) for a wholesale rout; fighting them pack-by-pack in darkness is a losing damage race. Great tutorial enemy for teaching 'bosses have conditions, not health bars.'
rating_notes: Zababa floor: specialist-only recognition = 1, no worship = influence 1 — honest mythic-tier board rarity, collector appeal per canon.

epithets_traditional: the man-devouring bird of the mountain

symbols: shadow of wings over the village
animals: giant eagle (folk memory of the real, extinct Haast's eagle)

temperament: Apex season villain of the sky-boxes; picks contestants off the open board until someone builds a better lobby.
game_hooks: BOSS SKETCH — huge flying predator. Untargetable while airborne; snatch attack applies crushed (talon grip) to isolated contestants and carries them toward the eyrie; dive strikes inflict bleeding; the decoy-runner role accrues exhausted. Discoverable win condition straight from the myth (Te Hau-o-Tawera's fifty warriors): build a lattice/net trap from gathered materials, one contestant volunteers as live bait to lure the dive, wings entangle, grounded = vulnerable window. Clues come from survivor-NPC accounts in raided villages. Bonus texture: it is folk memory of a REAL animal (Haast's eagle, which hunted 230 kg moa) — the bestiary entry can say so.
rating_notes: Influence 1 (beast, no cult); recognition 2 — the Haast's-eagle association gives it modest documentary fame beyond specialists.

epithets_traditional: the hidden little people of the valleys

temperament: The overnight construction union: a fortress by dawn, paid in shrimp, and the whole crew walks off the job forever if one camera points at them.
game_hooks: EVENT/NPC SEED — 'Night Works': at dusk a posted price appears (a food stake, traditionally one shrimp per worker); pay it and a bridge, rampart, or fish-weir exists at dawn, opening a route or fortifying a hold. Twist rule from the myth: if any contestant OBSERVES the work in progress (camera-visibility check — delicious in a show about cameras), the structure is abandoned mid-build, forever. A run-long economy of trust: the one thing the audience-obsessed show cannot film.
rating_notes: Baba Yaga logic: folk class, influence floor 1; recognition 2 — a Hawaiʻi-tourism household word but thin globally.

variants: Ka Makau Nui o Māui (Māui's great fishhook — the Scorpius asterism)
epithets_traditional: the hook that caught the land

symbols: the Scorpius star line used in living Hawaiian wayfinding

temperament: A jackpot relic in the prize vault — the hook that caught continents, still baited with the last owner's blood.
game_hooks: ITEM SEED — God Relic (jackpot-rare per canon loot rules: remnants of belief, never stat sticks). 'Fish Up the Land': once per run, raise a terrain platform/causeway from any water or void tile, or hook-pull a distant object or enemy to you. Activation must be baited with the wielder's own blood — self-inflict bleeding to use (in Grey, Māui smears the hook with his own blood when his brothers refuse him bait). Personality axes read as relic resonance: it hungers for absurd catches (risk 5) and stays loyal to one wielder (fidelity 4).
rating_notes: Influence 1 (object, no cult — though its star line IS living wayfinding knowledge); recognition 4: 'Maui's magic fishhook' is globally recognized via Moana even though the NAME Manaiakalani is specialist — note Disney's specific glowing-hook design is protected IP.

epithets_traditional: the phantom army of the old chiefs

temperament: The old guard's honor procession through the oldest wing at 3 a.m. — eyes down, back against the wall; they don't stop for guests.
game_hooks: ARENA EVENT SEED — 'The Procession': at night intervals a spirit column crosses the arena on a fixed ancient route (drums telegraph it — audio-first design). Any contestant in the path holding line of sight takes ramping dissolution and is downed at full stacks; countered by breaking line of sight, prostrating (a stance action — great forced-humility TV), or an ancestor-tie perk that lets the column pass over you (the moʻokūʻauhau rule: kin are spared). They are never fightable — a pure environmental sovereignty event. OWNER REVIEW before shipping: this is a living Hawaiian belief with kapu-adjacent protocol; census flagged it restricted — depiction respectful-or-not-at-all.
rating_notes: Influence 1 (feared, not worshipped; though belief is genuinely current in Hawaiʻi); recognition 2 via Hawaiʻi ghost-lore fame; restricted flag carried from census as data per the v2 ruling — collection is public-sphere (Pukui 1930 onward), depiction posture is the owner's call.

symbols: cap red on one side black on the other

temperament: Valet, bouncer, switchboard, and rigger of every doorway in the building, in a suit red on one side and black on the other; tip him going in or your chips, your messages, and your luck all reach the wrong table — a liability the house settled by making him a partner.

favor_conditions: pay a toll at the run's first door — leave an item or currency at the entrance tile before engaging anything
at a fork, take the unscouted route and clear what you find there
turn two enemies against each other and let them fight before you strike
declare your next move on camera and land it exactly as called

claim on camera that luck played no part in your win
shortchange a promised offering after the boon has already landed

game_hooks: Every table's front door is his franchise: entrance-toll mechanic seeds the floor's luck. As divine messenger he carries every god's bids between tables — bribable to misdeliver a rival's curse. He IS 'tip the dealer first' made flesh: natural dealer NPC. Event seed 'The Hat': a mid-run illusion shows each party member a different-colored objective marker; teams that compare notes pass, teams that quarrel take a feud debuff — his signature myth as a co-op trust test.
rating_notes: Rubric band 4 names Yoruba orishas (Amaterasu-consistent) = influence 4; mythology-curious tier (Enki band, census provisional 3 kept) = recognition 3.

temperament: The house actuary who read everyone's file before they were born; keeps the one ledger money can't buy, posts your survival odds without cruelty or comfort, and has never once gambled — nothing has ever been uncertain for him.

favor_conditions: obtain intel on a boss's weakness or win condition before first contact, for every boss on the floor
follow a received omen or read exactly as given, even when a better-looking option opens
end a run having visibly declined at least one high-variance gamble the audience goaded you toward
taboos: act against a warning you paid for (buy intel, then do the opposite)
misreport a read or the odds to your own team on camera
patron_block.blessing_style: pre-run disclosure — he already witnessed your destiny chosen; favor arrives as sealed intelligence: exact boss tells, trap locations, and one 'read' per floor that is always true

game_hooks: The house oddsmaker: his booth posts every contestant's live survival odds (the canon 1:259 board is his product line). The one being at the casino who already knows every player's numbers — his patronage is pure information. Forsaken all-ins get notarized at his desk with the opon Ifá as instrument. Diegetic honesty about the closed corpus: his verse library exists in-world as 'the one book the casino cannot buy' — never rendered, often coveted.
rating_notes: Core of living Ifá practice (UNESCO-listed system) = influence 4 per the orisha band; encyclopedia-depth outside practitioner circles = recognition 2.

epithets_traditional: Oba Ala (King of the White Cloth)

temperament: Immaculate white linen at the loudest party on the floor, sparkling water in hand — the sculptor who made every contestant on the board, sober ten thousand years over the one bender that mattered; he watches his imperfect handiwork get wagered on and calls the flaws his signature.

favor_conditions: win a run while carrying a permanently disabled body part from start to finish
protect or carry an injured or disabled contestant to the exit alive
clear a full run without using any intoxicant, frenzy, or judgment-clouding buff
end an encounter with zero kills where killing was on the table
taboos: consume palm wine or any drunkenness/frenzy-type consumable mid-run
mock or exploit a contestant's disability or disfigurement on camera
patron_block.blessing_style: cool white patience — slow, steady mending boons (body-part HP restoration, stabilizing auras) that never spike and never fail, and that strengthen the imperfect limb rather than replacing it

game_hooks: Patron god of every scarred, one-armed, prosthetic-limbed contestant build — his own myth makes imperfect bodies sacred (the GPT body-part HP system is mechanically native to him). Sponsors a 'sober run' challenge format (no buffs or intoxicants, higher multipliers). Story hook: the one table regular with visible remorse toward the players he shaped — played dry and morally alien per canon: his remorse is a craftsman's perfectionism, not kindness.
rating_notes: Living orisha band = influence 4; creation-myth staple known to mythology-curious audiences = recognition 3.

epithets_traditional: Oba Koso (The King Did Not Hang)

temperament: Arrives like a power chord — bata drums, red silk, double axe on the felt; a king who already went all-in on his own lightning once, lost the palace, the family, and the body, and converted the debt into godhood. Tips like money is for burning; strikes liars on live TV.

favor_conditions: strike a killing blow during an active storm, fire, or lightning field
drop two enemies with a single declared action
seek out and challenge the floor's strongest enemy before touching the rest
perform for the camera (dance, taunt, or hype action) immediately after a boss kill
taboos: lie on camera — the audience saw it, and so did he
steal a teammate's loot or kill credit
decline a duel you were formally challenged to
patron_block.blessing_style: spectacle-priced power — boons land as visible lightning charges on your weapon that double damage AND audience hype; the flashier the play, the bigger the next charge

game_hooks: FORSAKEN LENS: his own myth is an all-in loss — he tested his lightning charm, burned his own palace and family, hanged (or did not hang) at Koso, and converted total ruin into godhood. He is the casino's proof-of-concept that an erased loser can re-enter as a god (mortal-to-god pipeline, canon §3) — an eager, personally-experienced Forsaken host. Table gimmick: broadcast polygraph by lightning (his tables strike liars). Storm-arena boss format; bata-drum hype events. Gbonka-Timi myth = template for the house directing two NPC champions against each other.
rating_notes: Living orisha band = influence 4; recognition 4 (Changó's Latin-music and games/comics presence — Baba Yaga band); euhemeristic-king caveat carried from census for owner awareness.

epithets_traditional: Osin Imole (First of the Primordial Orishas)

symbols: railroad spikes and engine parts (modern cult)

temperament: The house's chief engineer and the only god in overalls: built the vault door, the shuffle machines, and every blade in every game; opens each new arena personally with one machete cut. Swears on iron because paper burns — and once cleared a whole town off the books in a single bad afternoon.

favor_conditions: be first through every breached door or obstacle on the floor
clear a run using only bladed or metal weapons
forge, repair, or upgrade a weapon mid-run and kill with it
open a shortcut or new route that other contestants then use
taboos: break an oath sworn on iron or on your own blade
retreat from a room you breached while objectives remain in it
patron_block.blessing_style: tools, not tricks — flat dependable edges: armor-piercing blades, doors that yield, machete-boons that clear terrain obstacles; nothing random, everything sharp

game_hooks: Diegetically HE cuts the path when the casino builds a new table — the house's arena contractor (his path-clearing myth is the opening cinematic of every new floor). Iron-oath notarization: contestant oaths sworn on a blade are enforceable contracts, breach triggers his wrath script (real Nigerian courts still accept oaths sworn on iron for Ogun devotees — irresistible diegetic detail). Hazard event 'Ire Protocol': a party that violates his observance day faces an unstoppable machete construct walking the floor — flee or appease, no damage race.
rating_notes: Living orisha band = influence 4 (active festivals, court oaths on iron); mythology-curious tier = recognition 3.

temperament: Honey-voiced VIP whose river fills the house fountains; the party runs on her guest list. Sweet past all accounting until she's left off one — then every tap in the building runs dry, floor by floor, until the apology airs live.

favor_conditions: heal another contestant while you yourself are below half health
resolve an encounter through charm or negotiation with zero damage dealt
give an item of real value to a poorer contestant on camera
end the run as the audience's most-loved contestant (top charm/hype rating)
taboos: offer or trade goods you know to be spoiled or broken
dismiss or talk over the party's least-imposing member in planning — the sixteen elders' mistake
patron_block.blessing_style: sweetness with a ledger — charm auras, audience-favor multipliers, and healing springs that flow freely until the moment she is slighted, at which point every tap on the floor runs dry at once

game_hooks: The audience-economy patron: her boons key off the hype meter (charm-multiplier patron archetype — mechanically paired with the shipped spectacle engine). Seventeenth-Orisha clause: a team that sidelines its support member triggers her floor-wide drought debuff (healing disabled) until a formal on-camera apology — her central myth as a literal mechanic. Osun-Osogbo grove (living UNESCO site) as her lounge skin — render respectfully.
rating_notes: Living orisha band = influence 4 (Osun-Osogbo festival, UNESCO grove); recognition 4 — Beyoncé-era pop visibility lifts her into the Baba Yaga/Ganesha band.

variants: Ṣọ̀pọ̀na (Yoruba name, traditionally taboo to speak)
epithets_traditional: Alapa-dupe (He Who Kills and Is Thanked)

temperament: Runs the basement table nobody names out loud — crutches, burlap, two loyal dogs, old scars under the lounge lights. The penthouse threw him out once; now every god upstairs quietly couriers tribute down, because his is the one table everybody plays eventually.

favor_conditions: carry an active infected or poison condition through a full floor and clear it without curing
give supplies or healing to the most injured contestant on the board, even an opponent
win a run you began at critical health or with a permanent injury
taboos: mock a diseased, scarred, or begging character on camera
speak his forbidden true name aloud on the broadcast
refuse aid to a downed contestant begging for it when you could give it
patron_block.blessing_style: the mark and the mercy — his blessing is a disease you keep: while marked (infected) you gain his resistances and his dogs' loyalty; cure the mark and the favor leaves with it

game_hooks: FORSAKEN LENS: the god the other orishas exiled — he wandered the roads as a scarred beggar and returned so feared and so loved that his real name went unspoken (and his cult was banned outright by the colonial state in 1907). He has already lost everything including his own name: a natural Forsaken host who runs basement plague-tables where the win condition is never 'kill' but 'endure/outlast.' Name-taboo mechanic: speaking his true name on broadcast summons a plague trial — a diegetic censorship bleep. NOTE for owner: primary name is deliberately the diaspora name per census sensitivity guidance.
rating_notes: Living orisha band = influence 4 (San Lázaro pilgrimages draw tens of thousands annually); recognition 3 — the NAME is pop-famous via Desi Arnaz's 'Babalú', the deity himself is mythology-curious tier.

temperament: Never leaves the flooded vault below the casino; half the treasury is technically his. Dresses better than the house, wears seven chains as jewelry, and has spent eternity pre-tilted about the one contest he lost — accepts any rematch, stakes no object.

favor_conditions: descend to the run's lowest reachable level and clear it
strip a flooded or underwater section of every piece of loot before surfacing
accept a rematch against an enemy or trial that already beat you, and win it
taboos: leave treasure behind on a depth level you entered
boast on camera that your winnings rival the house's
patron_block.blessing_style: abyssal escrow — boons surface slowly from the deep: sunken caches, pressure-proof endurance, and one rising flood per run he can send against your enemies; his gifts always arrive from below

game_hooks: FORSAKEN LENS: challenged the supreme god to a contest of splendor and was beaten by a chameleon that merely mirrored every cloth — a god of bottomless wealth carrying an eternal, self-inflicted grudge. Ideal Forsaken host: hosts abyssal-stakes rematches and would happily stake his own standing for one win over the house. Deepest-table format: his games flood floor by floor; hoard-boss economics (loot-gated, never kill-gated). Discoverable social win-condition for facing him: like the myth, he concedes to displays of effortless superiority, not to force. Androgynous/regionally varying gender — art direction note in dossier.
rating_notes: Living significant practice (Edo Olokun worship + Lucumí) = influence 4; encyclopedia-depth = recognition 2 — a rare-board VVIP: huge bankroll, low fame, collector's god.

temperament: The soft-spoken market matriarch cowried to the wrist who IS the money: never sits at a table, just drifts to stand behind whoever's winning — the dealers track her like weather.

favor_conditions: end the run with more currency than you entered with
trade with every merchant or vendor NPC encountered on the floor
turn a profit mid-run: buy or loot low, sell high before the exit
taboos: destroy sellable loot for spite or spectacle
steal from a market, shop, or trader NPC
patron_block.blessing_style: quiet compounding — no lightning, no drama: drop rates tick up, prices tilt your way, and an unexpected tiger cowrie in the loot means she has personally taken an interest

game_hooks: Casino nobility per spec §4 (luck_gambling flag): the pantheon's literal cash-flow deity. She doesn't host — she FOLLOWS winners: a roaming buff NPC whose presence multiplies loot, and whose departure is the floor's economic weather report. Mythic obscurity = rare boards and a collector's jackpot when she bids on a contestant. Her favor-sign (finding a tiger cowrie unexpectedly) is a ready-made rare-drop mark. PERSONALITY AXES PROVISIONAL — thin narrative corpus, see dossier.
rating_notes: Living but small/diffuse observance (Ọjọ́ Ajé Mondays, market veneration) = influence 3, honestly below the family ceiling; specialists only = recognition 1 (Zababa lesson: the floor is the floor).

temperament: A rerun legend on the memorial wall: the boy bred for one seven-night revenge run, his sorceress grandmother screaming plays from the rail — the tape ends with him standing against Smallpox itself while his mother treats the god like a head cold.
game_hooks: Legendary-contestant NPC and epic-reenactment chain: a vengeance ladder of named assassin-bosses (one per killer of his father), with a HANDLER twist — Oreame, a mortal sorceress, buffs him from the audience rail like a patron god, the casino's precedent for human 'coach' sponsors. Finale template: the Smallpox King arrives as a plague table-boss and the discoverable win is to treat the god's disease as an ordinary illness (cure/mundane-ize, never out-damage) — straight from the saga's ending, and a perfect non-damage-race boss per the hard rules.
rating_notes: Hero with no worship = influence 1 (Väinämöinen anchor); specialists only = recognition 1 — Väinämöinen minus the national-icon status.

temperament: Arena-rafter talent on a per-appearance contract: hangs over the canopy tables like bad scaffolding, iron teeth idling — the house loves that he works cheap and hates that he keeps eating the camera drones.
game_hooks: ENEMY SKETCH — size L elite/boss, arboreal ambusher. Hangs by backward-hooked feet from canopy/rafter tiles above the combat plane; swoop attacks inflict BLEEDING (iron teeth) and drag victims upward (suffocation pressure on grapple). Unreachable by melee while anchored. DISCOVERABLE WIN: (1) sever or burn its anchor branch/scaffold mid-swoop to drop it — grounded, its hook-feet make it slow and vulnerable to CRUSHED; or (2) learn from lore/NPCs that it enforces the forest rest-day: on the observance cycle it hangs dormant, the safe traversal window. Punishes players who treat the arena's 'sacred day' modifier as ignorable flavor.
rating_notes: Folk monster, no worship = influence 1; encyclopedia/bestiary-depth = recognition 2 — consistent with the Zababa floor and Baba Yaga's folk ceiling.

temperament: A pinprick of light drifting through the dormitory keyholes after lights-out; the house books it as 'ambient atmosphere' and quietly writes off a contestant a week.
game_hooks: ENEMY SKETCH — size S (light-mote) infiltrator, two-form design. Firefly form: untargetable, slips through door gaps/keyhole-class openings each turn toward the sleeping or lowest-HP contestant; its feeding applies BLEEDING + INFECTED (the folklore is a malaria explanation — disease-vector identity is native). DISCOVERABLE WIN: capture, don't chase — folklore states it reverts to a killable humanoid form only when caught: seal the room's gaps, then corner/grapple to force the transformation; killing attempts in mote form just scatter it. Event variant: an allied NPC is adze-possessed (possession-as-witch is core lore); the tell is envy-laden dialogue — expose the host instead of fighting.
rating_notes: Folk monster, no worship = influence 1; encyclopedia/bestiary-depth = recognition 2.

temperament: Eight hands, one credit line, and a royalty on everything ever broadcast — he sold the house its own story catalog after winning it in the wildest all-in on record (stake: his own mother). Bet him nothing you can't afford to become a story.
game_hooks: FORSAKEN LENS (folk-tier proof of the pattern): to buy all stories from the sky god he accepted an impossible four-capture price — python, hornets, leopard, forest-dwarf — and in the Rattray version threw in HIS OWN MOTHER as stake, then won. The casino's founding folk legend of the all-in that paid. Roles: dealer-commentator who owns the broadcast's rerun rights (every myth-reenactment epithet ceremony pays him a royalty — diegetic lore glue for the whole epithet catalog); contestant_legend whose signature run is a capture-gauntlet with a zero-kill win condition (the four-creature fetch as a mission template). Event 'Anansi's Price': floor-clearing intel offered for staking something living you love. Avoid modern-IP depictions (Gaiman's Mr. Nancy, Marvel) — folk substrate only.
rating_notes: Baba Yaga's calibration twin: folk being, never worshipped = influence 1; games/TV/children's-canon fame (American Gods, Gargoyles, school anthologies) = recognition 4.

temperament: The aquarium lounge's private financier — mirror, comb, python stole, impeccable contract terms: she'll bankroll a nobody into a star for silence and Saturdays, and her repossession department is the sea.

favor_conditions: keep your patron's identity secret through the whole broadcast — deflect every on-camera question about your backer
court no other god for the entire run (exclusive contract)
dive into water to rescue a contestant or recover loot and come out alive
maintain top appearance/presentation rating floor to floor
taboos: reveal or boast about her gifts on camera
accept a boon, bet, or side-deal from another patron while under her contract
patron_block.blessing_style: the covenant purse — sudden liquid wealth, magnetism, and healing while the pact holds; on breach, every payment reverses out of your account at the worst possible moment

game_hooks: The contract-patron archetype with a breachable exclusivity clause — temptation design: rival gods deliberately offer better boons mid-run to bait the breach (her jealousy is the mechanic). Her canonical image famously derives from an 1885 Hamburg chromolithograph of a snake charmer that African devotees adopted and re-divinized — diegetic gold for the canon thesis that gods wear what humanity imagines: she is the casino's proof that mortals co-author the gods' looks. Foreign-goods association (wealth arriving by ship) maps to import-crate loot events.
rating_notes: Continuous active local cults coast-wide plus diaspora, but not organized-orisha scale = influence 3; art-world and pop visibility = recognition 3.

symbols: carved face of Eshu on the rim

game_hooks: ITEM SEED — jackpot-rare God Relic (fits the 'loot must feel like remnants of belief' hard rule). Once per floor: declare one intended action; the tray dusts itself and marks the TRUE resolved outcome before you commit (deterministic sim preview — mechanically clean under the command-stream contract). Eshu's carved face takes his cut: each use costs an offering or a luck debit elsewhere on the floor. Render only the fully public iconography (tray, tapper, powder, geometric rim); actual odu verse content is initiation-closed and stays out of the game per the census closure note.
rating_notes: Artifact of a living UNESCO-listed practice — influence 3 records the practice's living weight, not worship of the object (semantics judgment call flagged in dossier); museum-level fame = recognition 2.

temperament: The house's retired champion brought back to preside — a table boss who'd rather judge your run than play it, crown catching the studio lights, always one dramatic sleep away from a comeback.
game_hooks: Legendary-NPC contestant / sleeping table-boss seed. Epithet templates: 'the Sword-Drawn' (win a run using a weapon you could only claim by proving worth — a locked/attuned weapon) and 'the Once and Future' (lose a run but bank progress that revives you stronger at a later table — the sleeping-king mechanic). As a table boss he presides rather than trades blows; the Forced-Action d6 failure path is his judgment. Never a damage race.
