LEGEND-FIRST call: the debated 5th–6th c. war-leader is NOT the entity — the LEGENDARY once-and-future king is (D-4 keeps the historical man out; the legend stays in). Recognition 5 + influence 1 = Normal-table collector's champion (fame alone does not raise table tier — Väinämöinen/Baba Yaga lesson).

temperament: A prophet-dealer working the floor in a starfield coat, sliding you tomorrow's card before you ask — brilliant, unbankruptable, and one seductive apprentice away from vanishing under the stage forever.

favor_conditions: learn a boss's win condition before you strike a single blow, and act on it
sacrifice a known advantage now (bank a turn, skip an easy kill) for a hidden one revealed later, and still win the run
reach a fork you were warned about and take the path you were told to take even when it looks the worse of the two
turn a marked enemy into an asset by recruiting or sparing them exactly as counselled
taboos: ignore a prophecy or plain warning he gave you and walk into the trap anyway
reveal Merlin's counsel to a rival patron, or squander his intelligence on a trivial fight
patron_block.blessing_style: cryptic pre-run intelligence and prophecy; boons arrive as foreknowledge (boss tells, safe paths, timed warnings) rather than raw stats — front-loaded information, the way he built a king out of a bastard.

game_hooks: Sorcerous/prophet PATRON. Also a natural dealer (he deals in fate) and a Forsaken-host candidate — a mage who staked everything and was undone, sealed alive under stone by his own student.
rating_notes: No worship, purely literary = influence 1; global household name (Merlin = the archetypal wizard) = recognition 5. Patron-capable as a sorcerous/prophet patron despite influence 1 — the fae/quasi-divine framing carries it. Normal table (fame ≠ tier). Demon-fathered but baptized (spirit-class: an otherworldly half-being, not an ordinary mortal).

temperament: The one player here with real worshippers in the stands — a fae-queen VIP who heals with one hand and poisons your rival's chips with the other, all smiles for the camera and knives for the recap.

favor_conditions: win a duel you could have avoided — take the pettier, more personal path to victory
steal or turn an enemy's own weapon/relic against them and finish the run wielding it
heal or revive an ally at real cost to yourself, then still close the run
humiliate a rival's champion on camera before you beat them
taboos: show mercy to someone Morgan has marked, or refuse a vengeance she offers you
court her rival the Lady of the Lake in the same run — the two fay will not share a table
patron_block.blessing_style: two-faced fae sorcery — a healing or shape-shifting boon that also poisons a rival's luck; gifts come wrapped in a grudge, and the price is always someone else's ruin.

game_hooks: Fae-sorceress PATRON (vengeful) and a VIP table-boss — the only entity in this tradition with live worshippers behind her. Forsaken-host candidate: her all-in vendetta streak (risk_appetite 4, pettiness 5) makes her stake big for spite.
rating_notes: Sets the tradition's influence ceiling at 3: the Glastonbury Goddess Temple (registered place of worship since 2003) venerates the Lady of Avalon = Morgen la Fey and the Nine Morgens — a small ORGANIZED REVIVAL (§3.1 influence 3, Ásatrú-scale). This REFINES the census's coarse 'living' flag down to the revival tier → depiction_risk 'low' (revival worship exists; depict respectfully), NOT 'living' (reserved for major/significant practice like Ganesha/Amaterasu). Under depiction v2 this is DATA, not a gate. Recognition 4 = games/comics/movies staple villainess. Influence 3 + recognition 4 = VIP (§3.5).

temperament: The house arms-dealer rising from the table's water feature to hand you exactly the weapon fate ordered — every gift a loan, every loan due back to the lake.

favor_conditions: recover a lost or sunken relic and return it to its rightful owner (or to the water) instead of keeping it
keep a sworn vow through a Forced Action — hold your word when the d6 tempts you to break it
raise a weaker ally into a champion — invest your run in someone else's rise and let them land the finishing move
accept a destined weapon, use it exactly as foretold, then surrender it back at run's end
taboos: keep for yourself a gift that was meant to be given onward or returned to the water
break faith with an ally she raised or entrusted to you
patron_block.blessing_style: an arms-dealer of fate — boons arrive as a perfectly-timed weapon, mount, or rescued champion handed up from the water; every gift is a loan against a destined return.

game_hooks: Fae PATRON — the arms-dealer of destiny: she hands out Excalibur, fosters and raises the champion Lancelot, imprisons Merlin, and ferries the dying king to Avalon. Also a dealer (she deals the weapon that decides the game).
rating_notes: No worship = influence 1; recognition 4 (the Excalibur-giving enchantress is broadly known). Patron-capable as a fae arms-dealer-of-destiny. Normal table. Note: Nimue/Viviane are scribal variants folded into one identity per Malory (first Lady unnamed, second named Nimue) — variants, not a cross-tradition syncretic group.

epithets_traditional: the Best Knight of the World

temperament: The circuit's undefeated golden boy, camera's darling, one forbidden romance away from being the scandal that ends the whole season.
game_hooks: Epithet-template legendary champion / rival-champion NPC. 'the Peerless' — win a duel against a numerically superior field without losing a body part. 'the Fallen Best' — a run where your greatest strength (the stat you invested most in) is the exact thing that dooms an ally, mirroring the love that broke the fellowship. Not patron-capable.
rating_notes: No worship = influence 1; recognition 4 (the archetypal greatest-knight / doomed-lover). Non-patron hero / contestant-legend. Normal table.

temperament: The queen in the royal box whose single glance moves more money than any bet on the board — the prize, the spark, and the fault line all at once.
game_hooks: Prize/queen NPC and love-triangle EVENT seed. Epithet 'the Kindling' — a run where a hidden relationship between two contestants raises the whole table's stakes then burns it down (the near-burning rescue is the set-piece). VIP-audience royalty; event-NPC, not a combatant.
rating_notes: No worship = influence 1; recognition 4 (the queen of the central love triangle). Non-patron prize/royalty NPC. Normal table.

temperament: The prime-time gladiator whose power meter visibly climbs to noon and drains after — a walking clock the crowd learns to bet around, and the one man who'll keep a deadly appointment on live TV.
game_hooks: Solar-champion epithet template — Malory: Gawain's strength waxes to noon and wanes after. Mechanic: a contestant whose power scales with an in-run clock (peak at the arena's 'noon' beat). Pairs with the Green Knight boss as the designated challenger. 'the Courteous' — win while honoring every social/oath obligation the arena throws at you.
rating_notes: No worship = influence 1; recognition 4 (Sir Gawain and the Green Knight is a school-syllabus staple; the 2021 film raised him further). Non-patron hero. Normal table.

temperament: The impossible clean-run kid the producers pray for and dread — flawless, untippable, and gone the instant he wins, raptured off-camera at peak ratings.
game_hooks: The 'perfect run' seed — a flawless/no-damage clear unlocks the Grail-tier reward and a mid-triumph 'rapture' exit (the run ends at its peak, banking maximum payout). Epithet 'the Sinless' — clear a Forsaken table taking zero taboo breaks. Ties to the Siege Perilous and Holy Grail artifacts.
rating_notes: No worship = influence 1; recognition 3 (known to the mythology-curious as the sinless Grail knight, below household-name tier). Non-patron hero. Normal table.

temperament: The inside man who turned the fellowship against itself — a boss you can't out-hit, only out-heal-the-schism, and he'll die on your blade taking you with him if you let him.
game_hooks: Table-boss / treachery-boss seed. Discoverable win condition (NEVER a damage race): Mordred cannot be beaten while the fellowship he has turned is still divided — the party must first re-unite the split/charmed allies (undo his subversion) before he is vulnerable. He and the champion can strike each other down at once (the Camlann double-kill), so the win condition is SURVIVING the mutual blow after the schism is healed, not out-damaging him. Conditions in play: bleeding, crushed, exhausted.
rating_notes: No worship = influence 1; recognition 3 (the archetypal traitor-son, a notch below Arthur/Merlin fame). Boss / table-boss material. Normal table.

variants: the Knight of the Green Chapel

temperament: The house's favorite wager: a holly-green giant who sets his own head on the table and dares you to swing — then invites you back for the return bet, grinning, to see if you'll stand still and honest when it's your neck.
game_hooks: BEHEADING-GAME BOSS — the showcase discoverable-win-condition boss, NEVER a damage race. The Green Knight offers a covenant: strike him once now, freely — he WILL survive (he picks up his own severed head) — and in exchange you must stand and take his return blow a round later without flinching or cheating. The 'fight' is not about damage: the win condition is to (a) land the opening blow, then (b) HOLD STILL and keep the covenant honestly at the return — flinching, dodging, or using a hidden charm (the girdle) FAILS the test; keeping faith reduces the return blow to a survivable neck-nick. A contestant who secretly stacks protection takes the wound; one who stands honest is spared. Conditions: bleeding (the nick), chilled/exhausted (the winter journey to the Green Chapel). He is ALSO a wager-host (luck_gambling): the nested 'exchange of winnings' is a second bet layered on the first.
rating_notes: No worship = influence 1; recognition 3 (SGGK and the 2021 film raised him, but short of household-name tier). Boss material + LUCK_GAMBLING flag — his beheading covenant and exchange-of-winnings are this tradition's clearest gambling resonance, making him casino nobility and a Forsaken-host archetype (risk_appetite 5, stakes his own head). Normal table.

temperament: The rare-board apparition every collector chases and no one bags — thirty hounds baying inside one impossible body, gone before the odds even settle.
game_hooks: Never-caught chase-boss seed. Size: LARGE chimera — serpent's head, leopard's body, lion's haunches, hart's feet; its belly bays like the noise of thirty couple of hounds (Malory). Discoverable win condition is NOT a kill — the Beast cannot be slain by damage (it always escapes; canonically no hunter ever bags it). The 'win' is to out-endure or out-track it: survive N pursuit phases, or trap it via terrain/lure rather than DPS. Its belly-noise is an audio TELL that telegraphs its next move. Affinities: exhausted (it wears hunters down), crushed. A collector's rare-board apparition.
rating_notes: No worship = influence 1; recognition 2 (encyclopedia-depth Arthurian bestiary — known to the curious, not the crowd). Rare-board beast. Normal table.

symbols: hand from the lake in white samite

temperament: The signature prize sword everyone films — but the seasoned know the real jackpot is the sheath that won't let you bleed, and the whole floor is watching to see who lifts it.
game_hooks: ITEM-SEED (attuned legendary weapon + the real prize is the SCABBARD). Sword: a high-tier blade that only functions for a contestant who has proven 'rightful' worth (attunement/worth gate; PLACEHOLDER numbers — e.g. must hold a table-boss title or pass a worth-check). SCABBARD (the greater treasure, per Merlin's own line): while worn, the bearer takes NO bleeding — negates the bleeding condition entirely (PLACEHOLDER: bleeding immunity while equipped). Discoverable drawback: the scabbard can be STOLEN mid-run (Morgan's theft) — a rival patron lifts it and restores your bleeding vulnerability for the finale, then throws it in a lake, gone. Thrown back to the water at run's end (a return-the-relic hook tied to the Lady of the Lake and Bedivere).
rating_notes: Artifact, no worship = influence 1; recognition 5 (Excalibur is a household word). Item-seed. Normal table (object placement follows the table system).

epithets_traditional: the Cup of the Last Supper

temperament: The untouchable pot at the center of the room — feeds everyone, belongs to no one, and burns the hand of anyone who reaches for it unclean.
game_hooks: ITEM-SEED — the jackpot / ultimate-reward: endless nourishment + healing, achievable only by the 'sinless' (a zero-taboo-break / perfect-run gate). Achieving it ends the run at its peak (Galahad's rapture) for maximum payout; approaching it while flawed is ruinous (it harms the unworthy — the false seekers wither). PLACEHOLDER numbers. IP/SENSITIVITY: PD literary object BUT entangled with living Christian eucharistic symbolism — OWNER REVIEW REQUIRED before any irreverent casino framing (census sensitivity note; depiction_risk marked 'low' for that reason, not for cultic worship of the object itself).
rating_notes: Artifact, no liturgical worship of the object = influence 1; recognition 5 (household word). depiction_risk 'low' flags the LIVING CHRISTIAN eucharistic symbolism entanglement (census note) — owner review before irreverent framing; under depiction v2 this is data, not a hard gate, but the flag is retained deliberately. Item-seed / jackpot. Normal table.

temperament: The fellowship table with no head seat and one chair nobody dares — sit the oath and the whole party wins together; break it and the felt goes cold.
game_hooks: ITEM-SEED — fellowship/party-buff and a diegetic pun (it can LITERALLY be the VIP gaming table). 150 seats, no head (Malory: Uther's table, gifted via Leodegrance as Guinevere's dowry, made by Merlin). Equality buff: while seated, party members share a pooled bonus and no single member draws 'leader' target priority (flat threat). The Pentecost Oath is a run-modifier: accept its constraints (give mercy, flee treason, succour ladies/damosels) for a fellowship multiplier; break it and the table's boon collapses. Contains the lethal empty seat — see the Siege Perilous. PLACEHOLDER numbers.
rating_notes: Artifact, influence 1; recognition 4 ('knights of the round table' is broadly known; the object itself a notch below Excalibur/Grail). Item-seed / party-buff. Normal table.

symbols: letters of gold naming the destined

temperament: The one empty chair on the Forsaken floor with your name maybe on the rim — sit worthy and take the whole pot, sit unworthy and the house takes you.
game_hooks: ITEM-SEED — the lethal empty seat; a cursed-relic / Forsaken-table hazard and a perfect all-in staking piece. Any contestant who takes the seat UNWORTHY is destroyed (death_underworld — Malory: fatal to anyone unworthy); only the one destined contestant (Galahad-tier: a perfect / zero-breach record) may sit, and claims a top-tier reward + the Grail-quest unlock. Discoverable TELL: the seat's rim reveals the destined name in gold (a worth-check readout). A gamble object — sit if you dare, bet your existence on being the worthy one. PLACEHOLDER numbers.
rating_notes: Artifact, influence 1; recognition 2 (known to Arthurian-curious + comics audiences via the X-Men 'Siege Perilous', but not a household term). Cursed-relic / Forsaken-hazard item-seed — strong all-in staking object. Normal table.

temperament: The majority shareholder that never leaves the top-floor office: three religions' worth of brand equity consolidated into one bankroll, moving markets by memo while its staff work the floor.
game_hooks: NEVER an on-screen character (sacred-core protection). Present only as an off-screen investor institution: the influence-5 bankroll that funds VIP tables, buys out champions mid-run, and issues the boons that arrive through messenger staff (§3.3 safe-default 'boons via intermediaries'). Canon hook: the three Abrahamic brands are segmented fronts of THIS one holding company — a market-share play. World-event flavor: a hostile buy-out of a rival pantheon's champion, executed by suits, never by the boss in person.
rating_notes: Figure of living major religions (>100M) = influence 5 per rubric anchor; the God-concept is a global household name = recognition 5. Represented per canon as the abstract holding-company investor (sacred core never rendered as a character), so it draws no table crowd despite recognition 5 — an off-screen majority shareholder whose favor is mediated by staff.

temperament: The Firm's head of security in a charcoal suit, sidearm holstered, badge clipped — polite until you break a house rule, then he is the last thing your run sees.

favor_conditions: clear a room without logging a single forbidden/rule-breaking action
take a hit meant for a downed ally to keep them standing
defeat an encounter's lesser guards before its commander — break the chain of command from the bottom up
taboos: flee an encounter you started and leave an ally behind
sign a rival demon's contract mid-run while carrying his badge
patron_block.blessing_style: escalating defensive detail — fight by the book and the Firm's security assets show up on your side; boons arrive as backup and hardened guard, not flourish.

game_hooks: Patron-broker for the Firm's protection/war portfolio: you never deal with the boss, you deal with the account manager who carries a gun. Recurring NPC as VIP-table head of security who enforces house rules on-camera — corporate enforcer, suit not armor, never haloed.
rating_notes: Actively venerated across Christianity and named in Islam with feast days and shrine cults, but no independent >1M worship base of his own = influence 3 (saint/folk-cult tier); a games/comics and 'biblically accurate angel' staple = recognition 4 (cf. Amaterasu recognition 4).

temperament: Corporate communications with a golden lanyard — every announcement, every camera-call, every 'and now, live from the arena' runs through this one press office.

favor_conditions: trigger a camera-call / spectacle event and then win the encounter it framed
land a decisive blow on the first turn you enter a new district (make an entrance)
complete an objective exactly as it was publicly announced, no improvisation
taboos: silence or skip a broadcast/announcement event
win by anticlimax — grind a boss down with chip damage instead of a decisive finish
patron_block.blessing_style: hype economy — boons scale with audience attention; a well-delivered Gabriel bulletin multiplies your next spectacular action and your tips from the stands.

game_hooks: Patron-broker for the Firm's spectacle/media portfolio; the in-fiction bridge to the audience economy. NPC role: press office that stages your camera-calls and reads out objective terms. Corporate presentation only — lanyard and mic, never wings-as-icon.
rating_notes: Venerated herald across all three brands with active devotion but no independent mass cult = influence 3; the broadly known announcement-angel = recognition 4.

temperament: Chief of staff and keeper of the ledger, the account manager seated closest to the boss's door; nothing happens on the floor that his pen doesn't record.

favor_conditions: discover and read a boss's win-condition text before engaging it
finish a run with a complete, unbroken action record (no hidden/unlogged moves)
solve an encounter through a written or numeric puzzle rather than force
taboos: destroy or skip a lore/record object (leave a page in the archive unread)
break a promise you logged earlier in the same run
patron_block.blessing_style: front-loaded intelligence — boons are information: the ledger shows an enemy's schedule, a hidden exit, the exact turn a trap fires; never raw stats.

game_hooks: The wisdom/intelligence patron-broker and the diegetic explanation of the HUD/ledger — his 'records' are the game's information layer. NPC: chief of staff who audits your run. Enoch-becomes-Metatron backstory is story-only lore (Enoch the mortal is NOT an entity, per D-4).
rating_notes: Esoteric kabbalistic figure with devotion thin outside mysticism = influence 2; known to mythology/occult-curious audiences and a recurring games/anime name = recognition 3 (cf. Enki recognition 3).

epithets_traditional: Keeper of the Ledger of Names

temperament: The closer from accounts receivable, immaculately courteous, who arrives exactly when your ticket says he will and never one minute early or late.

favor_conditions: land a killing blow on an enemy at the precise moment it is marked/scheduled (execute on the appointed turn)
let a doomed-flagged enemy expire on its own timer instead of spending resources to finish it
carry a fallen ally's token to the end of the run rather than abandoning the body
taboos: cheat a death the ledger recorded (revive past the appointed count)
kill out of turn — slaughter a surrendered or already-doomed foe for spite
patron_block.blessing_style: the appointment — Azrael's boons are certainties, not chances: a guaranteed kill on a scheduled target, but never a second past the hour.

game_hooks: Dealer who settles final accounts at a table (tips buy timing, not force) and the death/timer patron-broker. Great as the impartial functionary the desperate try to bargain with. Name is folk-tradition (not in the Quran, which uses Malak al-Mawt) — safely depictable as staff.
rating_notes: Widely invoked angel of death in folk Islam and Western folklore with some active folk practice = influence 3; a recognizable name to game/comic audiences = recognition 3.

temperament: The floor's most famous degenerate — went all-in against the house once, lost everything, and has been working the comeback ever since, grinning like the pot is already his.

favor_conditions: decline the safe reward and take the higher-stakes option at a fork
win a Forsaken (VVIP) game solo after refusing all mid-run help
beat an enemy that outclasses you on paper (win while flagged the underdog)
taboos: accept a buy-out from the Firm or a messenger after signing with him
cash out early / win small instead of pushing the pot
patron_block.blessing_style: leverage — boons are loans against your future: enormous power now, a debt that compounds; he front-loads the comeback and lets you carry the risk.

game_hooks: The archetypal FORSAKEN HOST (the original all-in bet, existence staked and lost — §5). Boss sketch: NOT a damage race — Lucifer's arena is a rigged escalating wager; the discoverable win condition is to out-gamble him: refuse each doubling deal until his pride makes HIM over-commit, then the house collects the collateral (his own light). Condition affinities burn / dissolution as his radiance turns on itself. luck_gambling flag; forsaken_candidate flag.
rating_notes: No mainstream worship — theistic Satanism/Luciferianism is fringe (<1M) = influence 1; the Devil is a global household name = recognition 5. CALIBRATION: like Baba Yaga (inf 1/rec 4→normal) and the Zeus lesson (fame ≠ VVIP), recognition-5 fame with zero worship-wealth lands normal-tier — a spectacular Forsaken/boss figure, not a rich patron.

epithets_traditional: Lord of the Heavenly Dwelling (Ba'al Zebul)

temperament: Old money gone rancid: a demoted god who still throws around a fortune that smells of decay, buzzing with a retinue that eats what it touches.

favor_conditions: win an encounter by attrition — let poison or infection do the finishing work
turn an enemy's own numbers against it (make a swarm or summon kill its master)
hold a chokepoint for a set number of turns without yielding ground
taboos: cleanse or cure a condition you inflicted (waste the rot)
keep a clean, tidy killbox — refuse to let the field degrade
patron_block.blessing_style: spread — boons are contagions: buffs that multiply across a swarm and creep onto whatever you touch, strongest in a filthy, condition-soaked arena.

game_hooks: Boss sketch (swarm-attrition): Beelzebub is immune while his fly-cloud feeds on a filth-source; discoverable win condition is to locate and burn the corpse/refuse pile that spawns the swarm (fire), starving him — never out-DPS the cloud. Field conditions infected / poison / suffocation. Also a fine Forsaken host (a bankrupt ex-god). forsaken_candidate flag.
rating_notes: A demoted Philistine god (Ba'al of Ekron, 2 Kings 1) with no surviving worship = influence 1; 'Lord of the Flies' is broadly known across literature and games = recognition 4.

temperament: The contractor who built half the tables and remembers every load-bearing flaw — hands you the blueprints with a smile and dares you to lose while holding them.

favor_conditions: exploit an arena's structural flaw (use terrain or a hazard to win instead of raw force)
win an encounter without logging a single wrath-triggered action (stay cold)
complete a build/crafting objective to the exact spec
taboos: smash a mechanism you were meant to operate
let anger pick your target — attack whatever provoked you first
patron_block.blessing_style: engineering — boons are tools and blueprints; the demon who built the table hands you its schematics, so your power is using the room, not overpowering it.

game_hooks: Enemy/dealer with a rage-scaling gimmick: he grows stronger every time the player attacks in wrath, so the discoverable counter is to fight cold and use the arena he built against him (crushed via structural collapse; burn from the forge). Solomon-cycle material is IN via Asmodeus/Seal of Solomon/Shamir; Solomon the person stays OUT (D-4).
rating_notes: No mainstream worship = influence 1; a fixture of games and demon lore (D&D, Shin Megami Tensei) and the Tobit/Testament of Solomon cycles = recognition 4.

temperament: The one name on the floor no manager can put on an org chart — she walked out of the first arrangement ever offered and has been unbossable ever since.

favor_conditions: win a run entirely solo, refusing every companion or summon offered
escape a trap or cage encounter instead of fighting through it (fly, don't submit)
fight and win in a darkened / night-flagged arena
taboos: accept a subordinate role in a scripted hierarchy (willingly take orders from a table_boss NPC)
harm a child- or helpless-tagged NPC for reward
patron_block.blessing_style: sovereignty — boons make you ungovernable: escape, evasion, and night-power that grow the more you refuse to be controlled; nothing that binds you to another patron.

game_hooks: Boss sketch (ward-not-force): Lilith cannot be beaten down; the discoverable win condition is to recover and inscribe the protective ward of the three angels (Sanoy, Sansenoy, Samangelof, from the Alphabet of Ben Sira) to bind her, echoing the amulet legend. Field conditions chilled / bleeding. Modern feminist/Wiccan/Luciferian veneration is why she carries 'living'.
rating_notes: Small but organized modern veneration (Wiccan, feminist-spirituality, Luciferian) — the most-worshipped folk demon today = influence 2; a AAA-game headliner (Diablo IV) and broad cultural figure = recognition 4.

symbols: gilded scales tipped by a thumb
downcast gaze (always looking at the floor for gold)

temperament: The deepest pockets on the floor and the shallowest loyalty — backs whoever's up, folds on whoever's down, and calls it good business.

favor_conditions: end a run with the largest treasure haul on the board (top the greed leaderboard)
buy your way past an obstacle instead of fighting it (spend, don't sweat)
win a bidding war for a contested reward against a rival
taboos: give away loot for free (hand a reward to an ally without payment)
leave a treasure uncollected when you could have taken it
patron_block.blessing_style: liquidity — boons are purchasable and instant, the deepest pockets on the floor; but he backs only whoever's winning and drops a losing book without sentiment (buy-out magnet — lowest fidelity on the roster).

game_hooks: The casino's native demon — 'the only fiend every casino already worships without knowing.' Dealer/patron who models the fidelity=1 buy-out mechanic (sells your contract the instant your odds dip). Enemy variant: a greed-trap boss immune while treasure remains on the field; win condition is to leave the hoard untouched (every coin taken empowers him; crushed via burial in gold, exhausted).
rating_notes: No worship (a personification of greed, Matthew 6:24; personified by Milton and Spenser) = influence 1; broadly recognized 'god of money' = recognition 3.

epithets_traditional: the Sea-Monster God Made to Play With
the Banquet at the End of Days
King over the Children of Pride

temperament: Not a gambler — the table itself when the game is the sea; the house wheels it out when a champion needs to be reminded how small they are.
game_hooks: Colossal boss / arena-beast (size class: leviathan). NOT a damage race — Job 41 frames it as unkillable by force ('canst thou draw out leviathan with a hook?'), so the discoverable win condition is environmental: recover the great Hook, drain/plug the flooding arena, or bait it into its counterpart Behemoth so the two slay each other (the end-of-days banquet myth, Bava Batra 74b). Field conditions suffocation (the flood) / chilled (the deep) / crushed (the coils).
rating_notes: A monster, never worshipped = influence 1; a household sea-monster name across games and film = recognition 4 (rubric anchors 'dragons' near the top of recognition).

temperament: The saint the desperate pray to when every other patron has passed — generous to the outlaw and the orphan alike, and merciless to anyone who breaks a promise to her.

favor_conditions: take a champion flagged doomed / lowest-odds into a Forsaken game and survive
keep a vow made to her for the entire run (honor a self-imposed rule to the letter)
finish the strong and the weak enemies alike, sparing none for reward (show death no favoritism)
taboos: break a promise sworn to her on camera
court a rival patron while carrying her favor (she is the jealous saint)
patron_block.blessing_style: the impartial hand — boons come to anyone who asks sincerely, outlaw or saint; generous and unconditional up front, then withdrawn with terrifying speed the instant a vow is broken.

game_hooks: Highest-influence folk patron and a natural FORSAKEN HOST — the saint of the nothing-to-lose, perfect for the doomed-champion frame (§5). Devotional lore drives her extreme personality (generosity 5 to the faithful, wrath 5 / jealousy on a broken vow — Chesnut). luck_gambling flag (petitioned for fortune). Carries 'living' (10â€“12M devotees, Church-condemned) â€” doubly sensitive; depict respectfully.
rating_notes: 10â€“12M living devotees (Chesnut, verified via Patheos/academia) = influence 4, matching the Amaterasu anchor; broadly recognized via news, tattoos, and TV = recognition 4.

temperament: A folk saint on a barstool, cigar lit and glass full, who will fix your luck and greenlight your revenge for the price of an offering the other patrons would call vulgar.

favor_conditions: spend or offer a consumable ('vice' item) before an encounter and then win it
win by trickery or an ambush rather than a fair fight
take revenge on an enemy that previously defeated you (rematch and win)
taboos: arrive empty-handed — court his favor without an offering in the log
moralize — refuse a dirty-but-effective tactic he offered
patron_block.blessing_style: the crooked favor — plied with offerings he tilts luck your way and greenlights tactics other patrons forbid; cheap to court, but the help is always a little crooked.

game_hooks: Low-recognition, rare-board patron with high collector appeal (obscurity 'rare'): the offering-driven luck patron who explicitly grants revenge and success-at-others'-expense (verified petition profile). luck_gambling flag. Carries 'living' AND 'restricted' â€” only widely-published public-sphere material used; the cofradÃ­a's ritual specifics (Santiago AtitlÃ¡n) are community-held and NOT collected. Owner-gate advised.
rating_notes: Active Tz'utujil cofradÃ­a practice, regional in scale = influence 3 (folk-saint cult tier); little recognition outside Guatemala/travel media = recognition 2 â†’ rare board, collector appeal.

epithets_traditional: the Being Whose Off-Switch is an Edit

symbols: Hebrew word emet (××ž×ª) on the forehead

temperament: House muscle with an off-switch you can read: a clay guardian that runs on a written word, unstoppable until someone edits it.
game_hooks: ITEM/CONSTRUCT SEED: a summonable clay guardian-ally whose 'durability' is a written Name (emet / ××ž×ª on the brow, or a shem-slip in the mouth). Signature mechanic straight from the legend â€” its off-switch is an EDIT: erase the aleph and emet ('truth') becomes met ('dead') and it collapses to inert clay. Failure state: if the Name is defaced by an enemy mid-fight it goes berserk and turns on its summoner (crushed damage; no bleeding/poison â€” it is unfeeling clay). Rabbi Loew the maker is story-only lore, NOT an entity (D-4 real human).
rating_notes: An artifact/construct, no worship = influence 1; a household concept across games, film, and modern AI discourse = recognition 4 (cf. Baba Yaga folk-class, normal tier despite high recognition).

epithets_traditional: he for whom the table is set

temperament: The oldest patron in the room and the most literal â€” the god you set a table for before the word 'casino' existed, who deals in nothing but the tilt of the odds.

favor_conditions: win an encounter decided by a coin-flip / low-odds outcome (let the gamble land)
set an offering at a shrine or table before a run and then complete the run
take the riskier of two offered paths and clear it
taboos: hedge every bet â€” never take an offered high-risk/high-reward option across a whole run
curse your luck aloud after a bad outcome (blaspheme against fortune)
patron_block.blessing_style: pure fortune â€” boons are reweighted dice: Gad doesn't make you stronger, he makes the improbable break your way; the casino's oldest and most literal patron.

game_hooks: The purest luck_gambling patron in the corpus â€” casino nobility despite the honest-floor scores. Mythic obscurity = rare on the boards, a collector's thrill when Fortune himself bids (canon rule). Flavor is literal: Isaiah 65:11 condemns apostates who 'set a table for Gad (Fortune) and fill mixed wine for Meni (Destiny)' â€” i.e. the first gambling shrine, condemned by the very Firm he now works alongside. Pair with Meni (Destiny) for a two-croupier event. luck_gambling flag.
rating_notes: Extinct pan-Semitic luck cult, remembered by specialists = influence 1, recognition 1 (the honest floor, cf. Zababa) â€” mythic obscurity means rare boards and collector appeal, not padding.
